observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the: 

(a) Municipal Treasurer (i) to furnish the updated RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to Municipal Accountant every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and 

(ii) to intensify and strategize tax collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places, and tapping the assistance of the barangay officials for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing updating of RPTARs

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the: 

a) officers and employees in charge of the implementation of projects funded by other agencies to (i) act swiftly in identifying priority projects that are acceptable to the SAs which are within the parameters of Memorandum of Agreement/Undertaking (MOA/MOU), taking into account the time frame of implementation of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of public service to the intended beneficiaries; and 

(ii) make representation to the OP for the immediate action to be taken by the LGU whether to still utilize or return the ₱5,000,000 fund, as well as the unutilized funds totaling ₱1,210,000.00 from two NGAs received in previous years; and 

b) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to practice the prompt liquidation of fund transfers including the immediate refund of unutilized balance of the said funds.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the i) LDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMFIP in the format as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002; and 

ii) Municipal Accountant to present the minimum required disclosure in the NFS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE direct the Accounting Office and the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO) to exert extra time and effort to gather pertinent documents or records to trace, account, and establish the correct year-end balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately make all necessary adjustments or remit all premiums due, when the evidence so warrants.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CSWMB to review and update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the City every two years or as the need arises, in compliance with Section 4(g), Rule VI of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Prepared Not According to the City Environment and Natural Resources Officer, the process of updating the City’s software will be coordinated with system administrator.
observations.reason: According to the City Environment and Natural Resources Officer, the process of updating the City’s software will be coordinated with system administrator.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Acting City Treasurer to: a. ensure that the payrolls are duly certified and approved by respective City Officials and Employees prior to drawing of checks; b. ensure that the amount of cash advance for the payment of salaries, wages, allowances and honoraria are equal to the net amount of payroll for a pay period; c. strictly adhere to Section 5.1.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and d. prepare the Report of Disbursements and forward the same to the City Accounting Office as part of the liquidation documents of the cash advances pursuant to Section 49 of the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I. We also recommended that the City Accountant to conduct periodic review of liquidation documents and ensure recording of liquidations on actual dates and that the City develop measures to impose penalties for non-compliance with COA Circular provisions.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and City Treasurer coordinated and exerted efforts to reconcile and to ensure payrolls are certified. Duly certified payrolls were exerted to ensure correctness. City Accountant required submission of liquidation documents. Some DVs and financial transaction supporting documents were submitted within the reglementary period. The City partially adopted Automated Payroll System (APS) for Job Order payment.
observations.reason: Inadequate review of payroll hindered the full implementation of the audit recommendation. The same condition was still observed during the conduct of the audit in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to: facilitate the proper turn-over of the constructed infrastructure assets on barangay-owned lots to the respective recipients; and ensure that the corresponding costs are recognized in the recipients’ books. Henceforth, turn-over the constructed infrastructure assets on barangay-owned lots and recognize the corresponding cost in the recipient’s books.
observations.managementAction: The City General Services Office coordinated with the City Accounting Office to verify documents necessary for the recognition and de-recognition of cost in the City’s book of accounts. Verified available documents of projects and updated records. The IC is still in the process of identifying structures to be turned-over.
observations.reason: The IC is still in the process of identifying structures to be turned-over to other government entities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to: review and analyze the PPE subsidiary record and establish the actual cost of each property item, taking into consideration the cost elements of constructed assets as provided for under Sections 14 and 30 of IPSAS 17; and make the necessary adjustments to accounts. Henceforth, comply strictly with Sections 14 and 30 of IPSAS 17 on cost recognition of PPE, as well as Sections 111 and 112 of PD No. 1445 in the maintenance of subsidiary record for each PPE.
observations.managementAction: Both City General Services Office (CGSO) and City Accounting Office (CAO) are still reviewing and gathering documents necessary for the de-recognition and recognition of cost in the City’s book of accounts. On-going analysis of recorded items under infrastructure assets.
observations.reason: Analysis of recorded items under infrastructure assets is still on-going. Implementation of recommendation is dependent on the result of the on-going analysis of recorded items under infrastructure assets. Lack of manpower for maintenance of subsidiary records hindered the full compliance with the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer to: (a) exert effort to locate pertinent files and gather relevant information as basis in setting up the proper RPT/SET Receivable/Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable and corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of the year; (b) keep and maintain detailed records, specifically the collections and subsequent recording of the RPT/SET income per real property classifications; and (c) analyze and investigate the cause/s of the deficiency of balances noted by the team between the RPT and SET Receivables and the Certified List of Delinquent Taxes extracted from the Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) as of December 31, 2022 and make the necessary adjustments in their records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Assessor’s Office is in the process of updating the certified list of RPT tax delinquency produced by the PATAS with the balance reflected in the Accounting record. On-going analysis and reconciliation of records is being done.
observations.reason: Evaluation and analysis of the discrepancy is still on-going. No reasoned information was provided to the Audit Team. Management was not able to strictly comply with the laws, rules and regulations because of the time needed to reconcile records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) within the period prescribed under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Item 3.2 COA Circular No. 96-011; the City Accountant and the Acting City Treasurer to trace the material variance of P380,524,400.27 between the SLs and Cashbook balances and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment to accounts; the City Accountant and City Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of their balances; and that the City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer provide a written justification for the repeated non-compliance, otherwise administrative sanctions shall be imposed pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant prepared and submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements although some were submitted late due to personnel constraints. On-going reconciliation between City Accounting Office and City Treasurer’s Office is being conducted. RTS for the above recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.
observations.reason: Delays in preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements occurred due to personnel assigned for the preparation being on leave/medical reasons. Considering recurring nature, the City Accountant needs to revisit work setup; BRS preparation delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to observe caution in recording transactions by conducting adequate review of reports from source documents, so as to record transactions on actual dates and produce accurate records/reports.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the City Accountant. Accountable Officers reports, specifically reports by collectors, were partially reviewed. The City Treasurer expressed concurrence with the recommendation. As an update, she informed that proper recording of transactions for the current year is being practiced.
observations.reason: Instances of deposits not recorded on actual date of occurrence are still being observed. Inadequate review of reports by collector and by the City Accountant contributed to non-compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, henceforth, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to religiously prepare the monthly trial balance supported with the SAAOb and submit the same on or before the 20th day after the end of the month pursuant to Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction:
As evidence of Management’s compliance, the Municipal Accountant submitted the monthly trial balance from January to March of CY 2023 and the quarterly Financial Statements for the 1st quarter of CY 2023.
The Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) is in delay in submitting the monthly Pre-Closing Trial Balances (Pre-CTB) and Interim Reports for Calendar Year (CY) 2023, by 9 to 333 days.
observations.reason: The GSMS will be updated to include a feature allowing editing of data components for the finalization of POs once details are extracted from the Purchase Requests (PRs).

observations.recommendation: We recommended, henceforth, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that POs should contain the brand names together with the specifications of the items pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: The Management, through the Municipal General Services Officer, explained that the Municipality’s General Services Management System (GSMS) does not have a feature that will show the editing of components for the finalization of POs once details are extracted from the Purchase Requests (PRs).
observations.reason: The GSMS will be updated to include a feature allowing editing of data components for the finalization of POs once details are extracted from the Purchase Requests (PRs).

observations.recommendation: We recommended, henceforth, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer/Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee to submit copies of Contracts, POs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five working days after its perfection/issuance in compliance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, (CY) 2023, Copies of 14 Contracts and 64 Purchase Orders, totaling P29,811,928.10 and P76,125,780.25, respectively, were still not submitted within Five working days from the date of execution and/or issuance; hence, the audit recommendation was not addressed by the Management.
The Municipal Mayor issued Memorandum No. 053 and 053-A Series of 2024 directing the BAC and MGSO to timely submit copies of Contracts, delivery documents, POs and its supporting documents within the prescribed period set by the Commission. Furthermore, the OIC – Municipal General Services Officer explained that the delay was caused by the demise of the Municipal General Services Officer. During the time of delay, the Municipality still awaits the appointment of officer-in-charge who will lead the overall affairs of the MGSO. However, the Management agreed to the audit recommendation on timely submission of the needed documents including inclusion of brand names in the POs.
observations.reason: The municipal offices delayed submission due to institutional issues (demise of the Municipal General Services Officer and awaiting appointment of officer-in-charge) and thus documents remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation:
Management, through the General Services Office, initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 162 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

Management submit the RPCPPE, IIRUP, and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Semi-Expendable Property (IIRUSP) in the prescribed format in accordance with the templates provided in COA Circular Nos. 2002-003 and 2022-004.

Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, initiate the disposal of the obsolete, spoiled, and cancelled checks in accordance with the requirements and procedures in Section 86 (H) of the LTOM 2nd Edition.

Management, through the Municipal Assessor, complete the preparation and updating of FAAS for the remaining 11,391 real property units simultaneous with the ongoing general revision of the real property tax. Further, revisit its existing staffing requirements and expedite its request for additional staff, if warranted and if the budget allows, to ensure that all subject properties are completely supported with duly accomplished and approved FAAS in accordance with the provision in the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations.

Management institute proper remedial measures to ensure that the PPAs in the GPB are monitored to seek immediate solutions for the unexpected events, and be implemented timely to attain the GAD objectives pursuant to the provisions of PCW DILG DBM NEDA JMC No.  2013-01 and 2016-01.

Management make representation with the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on the afore-mentioned deficiency and to expedite the establishment of its website to avoid non-compliance of similar requirement.

Mnagement submit its legal basis on the inclusion of lot acquisition under the build and design scheme for evaluation by this office and to appropriately make manifestation with the National Housing Authority on the noted deficiency in the aforementioned terms of reference.

Management should limit the granting of additional benefits for personal services within the 55% level for a fourth-class municipality and lower as provided for by law and to require all officials and employees to refund the amount of ₱10,000.00 each. 

Furthermore, any review comments by the provincial finance committee on municipal budgets that needs to be complied with strictly must be taken into consideration before disbursing funds to avoid disallowance in post-audit.

Management should limit the grant of additional benefits for personal services within the 55 per cent level for a fifth-class municipality and lower as provided for by law and  require all officials and employees to refund the amount of ₱10,000.00 each. 

Furthermore, any review comments by the provincial finance committee on municipal budgets that need to be complied with strictly must be taken into consideration before disbursing funds to avoid disallowance in post-audit.

The ICO-Municipal Treasurer should be required to restitute immediately the missing funds.

Municipality observe the provisions of Sections 123 and 124 of P.D. 1445.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit to the National Treasury the unutilized PDAF amounting to ₱121,680.00 pursuant to afore-cited SC decisions. 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment to properly classify the financial assistance from PDAF to its appropriate account by debiting Due to Philheatlh and crediting Due to National Government Agencies (NGAs)- BTr.

The Local Chief Executive coordinate with the LDRRM Office to ensure that all PPAs included in the LDRRM Plan are implemented and LDRRMF utilization are maximized to attain its goals for the benefit of its constituents.

The LDRRM Officer, as the one who prepares the LDRRMFIP and requests the utilization of the fund, to ensure that PPAs are included in the LDRRMFIP and with approved AIP before utilizing the LDRRMF pursuant to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

Submit a written justification on the deficiencies noted and take immediate action to rectify/correct the deficiencies. 

Submit concrete proof thereof, for evaluation by the Audit Team, otherwise, a Notice of Suspension/ Disallowance for the total project cost may be issued thereon.

Submit documents that can support the appropriation for the payments of overtime services which exceeded the budget.

Monitor the budget appropriation for each line item to avoid recurrence of budget overdraft;

Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer (MHRMO) to monitor the overtime hours of the personnel so as to prevent the payment of overtime services in excess of the limitation and submit Overtime Work Program to establish necessity of such.

Submit their quantified accomplishment reports.

Designate personnel or create a committee who will facilitate the titling of these parcels of land and allocate funds thereof, in order to intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the Municipality.  

Establish the existence of these three lots with tax declarations in the name of the Municipality, and determine how these lots were acquired by the LGU.  Thereafter, record accordingly in the books of accounts pursuant to the recognition criteria provided under Paragraphs 26 and 27 of IPSAS.

The Municipal Accountant to work on the unidentified item in the accounts with no details to provide any supporting documents to establish the balance and consider the applicability of the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2016-005 so that appropriate action can be done for its final disposition.

Return unspent balances of financial assistance received after implementation of the projects pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 or secure authority from source agencies to use the balances for other priority projects.

The accounting unit to file the request for authority to write-off for dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
The accountant consider recording the provision of Impairment Loss and corresponding Allowance for Impairment to set up provision for losses arising from non-collection or non-liquidation of receivables.

Make a follow-up with PAO, and PDIC to fast-track the resolution of the case and the filing of appropriate charges against the persons found to be liable.

Communicate with Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to determine the status and seek assistance for the recovery of the cash deposit.

Commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU.

Expedite the implementation of 29 projects out of financial assistance received pursuant to the respective Memorandum of Agreement, Trust Agreement or Memorandum of Understanding which governs the utilization of funds and conduct regular monitoring and analysis to ensure that fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose.

We recommended that the PPE Inventory Committee: 1. Re-evaluate the scheduling of its physical inventory count to ensure completion before the financial statement submission deadline; and 2. Expedite its procedures in the conduct of physical count of inventories, and timely submit the required reports such as the RCPPE, ICF, List of PPEs found at Station and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs, PIP.
observations.managementAction: Management had already conducted an inventory of all its properties. However, they still had to reconcile the balance of the actual count against the balance reported in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to evaluate the feasibility and closely monitor the implementation of the various PPAs in accordance with the approved development plan as required under Section 476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160. In addition, we recommended that Management consider all possible limitations, including manpower and availability of equipment, during the planning stage.
observations.managementAction: Among the reasons for the non-implementation of the majority of these projects include the price escalation of fuel and construction materials, the temporary suspension of the quarry in the Province, and the lack of heavy equipment. The Municipal Engineer, however, commented that in 2024, they will be purchasing heavy equipment to aid in fast-tracking the implementation of these projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the BAC first secure the appropriate authority to purchase motor vehicles in compliance with DBM BC No. 2022-1 and DILG MC No. 2022-105.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team that those without APMV were transactions that happened prior receipt of the Audit Team’s advice pertaining the requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Sanggunian facilitate the review and evaluation of its current tax and revenue sources and assess whether the 2011 Revenue Code of the Municipality of Santa Catalina, Negros Oriental is still up-to-date with the current condition and collection capability of the Municipal Government. Otherwise, the Municipal Mayor and Sanggunian shall update and revise the existing Code, incorporating all other sources of revenue. We also recommended that the appropriate MOAs be executed for the rest of the four tourist attraction sites by the Municipality to the corresponding Barangays concerned, community associations, and/or landowners.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Local Revenue Code had been updated last CY 2017 and submitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for approval but was returned to corrections not yet complied. The Local Chief Executive, however, issued Executive Order No. 24, series of 2023, for the creation of a municipal task force committee on the updating of the local revenue code of the Municipality. MOAs for four tourism attraction sites are still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System to: 20. Ensure that budget allocation for GAD is optimally utilized in providing adequate facilities and services to address gender issues and the GAD mandate of the LGU; 21. Prepare a more realistic and attainable GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to ensure that GAD projects and activities will be carried out in accordance with the approved plan; and 22. Establish an effective monitoring and evaluation system to monitor the implementation of the annual GPB.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that some of the proposed and approved PPAs under GAD include construction projects which are challenging to implement because of its limited number of personnel. For CY 2025, GAD Plan and Budget were already prepared. Monitoring and Evaluation Committee is already created but implementation and full M&E are not yet in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS prepare and set up the GAD Agenda of the Municipal Government and identify priority gender issues of the LGU for the next three-year term of leadership, pursuant to Section 4, Item C.2 (1) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1, as amended.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2023 had already been prepared and submitted. However, the GAD Agenda still had to be prepared and submitted once available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities: 25. Conduct risk assessments considering new variables after the pandemic; 26. Identify critical processes and functions; 27. Determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operations; 28. Conduct risk and impact analyses; 29. Formulate the PSCP; and 30. Submit the plan to the LDRRMO for review, to the LCE for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.
observations.managementAction: Management had already created a committee to formulate/prepare the PSCP, in accordance with Executive Order No. 13. However, said PSCP was not completed due to workload and other priorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 32. Establish an M&E System through the issuance of an Executive Order or Administrative Order; 33. Oblige the M&E Team, through its GFPS, to conduct an assessment and evaluation of all the gender-responsive or GAD-focused policies, programs, projects, accomplishments, and actual costs for the past three years during his administration; 34. Task them to provide recommendations based on its assessment and evaluation, when necessary; and 35. Ensure that the M&E Team consolidate the results of the assessment and evaluation in a “GAD Evaluation Report,” which shall be submitted to the DILG Regional Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still lacked the personnel necessary to form the M&E System. Besides, Management commented that it has a GFPS which also oversees and monitors the implementation of GAD projects and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in identifying the “For ID” accounts totaling ₱6,007,493.77 and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify these to the proper asset accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant admitted that these accounts cannot be traced. The said balances were carried forward from prior years of the Old Government Accounting System to the eNGAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operations of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No 111 dated June 10, 2016 to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management admitted that the preparation of the required Business Plan is tedious and would consume more time. Management commented that the New Market Code was already approved, hence, the Business Plan would be prepared and submitted as soon as it is finished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 42. The Municipal Mayor instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 so that the municipal government can still realize income therefrom. 43. The Municipal Treasurer hasten the preparation of the final list of unserviceable properties so that the cost thereof can finally be determined, and the book value reclassified to the Other Assets account while awaiting disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Appraisal and Disposal Committee had already prepared an updated list of unserviceable properties. These would be disposed as soon as the appraisal of these properties has been completed and means of disposal determined.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the latter promised to reconcile the accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments so that the RPT Receivable and the SET Receivable are of the same amount which are also equal to the corollary accounts Deferred RPT Income and Deferred SET Income.

1.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the latter assured the LCE and the Audit Team to intensify the collection of real property taxes and issue notice of delinquency as required in RA No. 7160 to all delinquent taxpayers for them to settle all their taxes due to augment income of the Municipality that could be used to finance priority programs and projects.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the latter pledged to locate or trace the documents of recorded infrastructure assets and make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct book value of the foregoing infrastructure assets, hence to present fairly and reliably the FS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the General Services Officer or the official responsible in accepting deliveries to furnish a notice to the Office of the Auditor on the deliveries of procured items within 24 hours from the acceptance to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 96-010. If personal submission of said notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the Inspection and Acceptance Report could be submitted thru the official email address of the Audit Team (lgsagbteam07@yahoo.com) to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items/goods to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed with the quantity, quality and specifications in the Purchase Order. Thus, in case of any discrepancies, Management be immediately informed for its appropriate and immediate action. Likewise, it is encouraged and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the latter assured to require the pictures of the items/goods delivered to be attached in the Disbursement Voucher.

4.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer and the latter promised to furnish the Auditor list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year. Also, provide a notice to the Office of the Auditor within ten days, after the award of infrastructure projects or before its start that the project signboard was already posted. If personal submission of said list or notice is not possible, the duly signed scanned copy of the list or notice could be submitted to the official email address of the Audit Team (lgsagbteam07@yahoo.com). Thus, enable the Auditor to conduct timely verification and monitoring of the implemented infrastructure projects and deficiencies, if any, would be communicated to Management for its immediate and appropriate action. More so, the constituents are also informed of such public concern, hence transparency for good governance is ensured.

5.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the latter ensured strict compliance on the abovementioned regulations pertaining to the submission of the monthly and quarterly trial balances, including the supporting SAOB, journals, ledgers and supporting schedules to facilitate review and verification thereon, and deficiencies if any, may be immediately communicated to management, for the latter’s consideration.

7.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the BAC and the latter assured to submit all the copies of the perfected contracts together with all the documents forming part thereof to the Auditor within five working days from the execution of contract as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and evaluation of compliance with the requirements of applicable laws, rules and regulations and completeness of documentary requirements thus, enable the auditor to inform the. Management of any deficiencies that may be observed for immediate and appropriate action.

8.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Inventory Committee and the latter promised to conduct annually complete physical count of all PPE and report the result of inventory in the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance with the provision of Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

8.6 Likewise, we recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GSO, Head of Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant and all of them promised to adhere with the guidelines and procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the conduct of physical count of properties; recognition of PPE items found at station; disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items; and for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, in order to reflect more reliable and accurate balances of PPE accounts, hence to convey precise information useful in decision making and accountability for these properties.

9.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) request from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to establish guidelines as to the mobile/ communication expenses being received by the department heads and to integrate such expense/ allowance into the payroll system;
b) instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer that the internet services per office be paid through checks directly to the supplier; and
c) direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the utilization of PCF, that the same shall be sufficient for the recurring petty operating expenses of the agency for one month. The cash advance shall not be used for payment of regular expenses, such as rentals, subscriptions, light and water bills and the like.

10.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the employee/s in charge with the approval of loans to refrain from granting loans and/or additional loans or renewal of existing loans of the concerned employees and/ or all others, by securing clearance from the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer, to ensure that such employees meet the minimum take home pay of ₱5,000.00 as prescribed under the GAA in order to boost employees’ morale which would ultimately redound to a more efficient and effective delivery of services to the general public.

11.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the end-user units and the BAC Chairman and both of them ensured to prepare the PPMP and APP, respectively, in compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 7 of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184, GPPB Circular 07-2015, and Volume 1 of the GPPB Generic Procurement Manual by using the GPPB-prescribed format and to properly accomplish the same by providing all the relevant information necessary in the efficient and effective implementation of planned PPAs, and this will serve as a tool/guide of the agency to optimally utilize their resources, hence to meet the needs of the constituents or end-users at a favorable time.

12.12 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Formulate or adopt policies and guidelines promulgated by DSWD on the grant of financial assistance under the AICS program to strengthen the controls employed in the selection of eligible beneficiaries to avoid inequitable allocation to the constituents of the Municipality; and
b) Establish a cash advance exclusively for AICS to facilitate the immediate release of the financial assistance to the beneficiaries in the form of cash payment thus, to address promptly their urgent needs and to alleviate their critical situations.

13.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC Chairman to instruct the BAC Secretariat and the latter ensured the posting NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS on the prescribed periods as required in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, in order to conform with the objectives of the procurement law in promoting transparency and accountability in the conduct of procurement activities.

14.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to request from the Sangguniang Bayan for a resolution to convert the payment of employees’ salaries, bonuses and other monetary benefits through ATM in order for the Management to strengthen its internal controls on cash and avoid the possible risk of theft, loss or misappropriation.

14.8 During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer said that their application request was already submitted to their Authorized Depository Bank for approval.

15.8 We recommend and the LCE agreed to:
a) instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Implementing Offices or focal persons to send notices/ demand letters to the concerned NGOs/POs for the immediate submission of liquidation report and/or collection and adopt other measures to recover the amount of fund transfers, such as seeking legal remedies, to allow the LGU of additional funds to finance more developmental projects.
b) require the Municipal Accountant to follow the procedures for the proper disposition and derecognition of dormant accounts as mandated by Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2023-008 in order to fairly present the FS.

16.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the latter promised to send demand letters for the immediate liquidation of the cash advances and ensure that the same is received by the concerned persons and seek legal remedies so that the unliquidated cash advances of those accountable persons who are no longer in service will be settled the soonest possible time.

16.10 Likewise, we recommended and the LCE agreed to remind the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer and both of them promised to observe compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445 for the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period and that no additional cash advance shall be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made to avoid misappropriation and possible loss of government funds. Non-observance of the above-cited rules on the liquidation of cash advances shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of salaries of erring accountable officers or employees.

17.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the refund of the amount of ₱25,000.00 from all concerned employees. Likewise, we recommended that any grant of financial assistance should be supported by compelling reason to justify the same in order not to spent the government funds for unnecessary expenditures which are not relevant to the objectives and mission of the agency relative to the nature of its operation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Submit the required Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment showing relevant information, such as property number, unit value, balance of PPE per property cards and per count; and
Undertake all other procedures prescribed under the same Circular to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible based on updated Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting up the Receivables from RPT and SET; and
The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the RPT/SET Receivables established at the beginning of the year based on the duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the reported balance of the affected accounts and ensure that the proper accounting and reporting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 111 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 are strictly complied with to ensure the fair presentation of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective of the fund, as well as the desired benefits, in compliance with Republic Act No. 10121.

We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from charging administrative expenses under the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-a dated 
February 22, 2017 in the utilization of the said Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed, thru the Local Chief Executive, to undertake the following courses of action:
Discontinue the practice of dropping items of PPE from the books of accounts without undergoing the proper process of disposal;
Strengthen internal controls pertinent to property accountability and clearance from accountability in order to safeguard government properties against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition with a view to ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of government; and
Consult with the PFDA on any construction to be made on the port site as stated in their Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommend that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to undertake the following:
Evaluate and determine the nature and status of the transactions recorded under the Other Payables - Trust Fund in order to come up with an appropriate action or disposition;
Draw the necessary journal entries to properly present the various transactions to its proper account; and
Ensure fair presentation of financial statements in accordance with Item 27 of International Public Sector Accounting Standard 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to undertake the following:
Submit the monthly and annual financial and statistical reports, together with all supporting papers, to the PFDA and remit the royalty fees due to PFDA subject to budgeting and accounting guidelines and procedures; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to recognize the liability for the Royalty Fees for CY 2004 to CY 2023, to ensure fair presentation of financial statements in accordance with Item 27 of International Public Sector Accounting Standard 1 and to comply with their contractual obligation with the PFDA under their Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the reported balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM account and ensure that the proper accounting and reporting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002, Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 are strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Review thoroughly the Check Registers from Treasury Office and identify all the unreleased checks at the end of the year.  Effect the necessary adjustment by restoring the Cash in Bank previously credited and set up Accounts Payable for the unreleased checks.  At the beginning of the ensuing year, reverse the adjustments made;
Verify the book reconciling items by inquiry to the servicing banks to determine the correctness of the additions and deductions made by the bank and to adjust the corresponding appropriate income and expenses accounts;
Cancel all the stale checks and make the necessary adjusting entries:
Cash In Bank				P809,244.41
Appropriate Payable Account		P809,244.41
Present the adjusted balance of Cash in Bank Account in the financial statement pursuant to the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Review the records of the Due from Other Funds account in the GF and Due to Other Funds account in the TF and trace the errors leading to the unreconciled balances from CY 2016 up to CY 2023, and post the necessary adjustments to present the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts;
Make a reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts every year-end to present an adjusted and reconciled balances in the Financial Statements; and
Stop using the SEF funds for GF expenditures and immediately facilitate the transfer of the amount of P184,550.39 to the SEF so that this will be readily available for the intended purpose of the fund, pursuant to Section 272 of the RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Make an appointment with the GSIS BO to identify the discrepancies and make necessary adjustments;
Identify the source of the over-remittance, and if warranted, demand from the GSIS the return of the excess payments made that were not credited to the employees; and
Ensure that the remittances or payments to GSIS are correct, proper and complete to safeguard the interest of the employees of the Municipality and pursuant to GSIS rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
instruct the concerned offices to submit the liquidation reports such as FURs, RCDs, RCIs to the different SA and to COA, following the provisions of Item 6.4 and 6.5 of COA Circular No. 94-013 to account the utilization of the funds transferred by the SA in their books, and to monitor the status of the implementation of the funded projects/programs/activities;
instruct the concerned offices to request from the SAs for authority to transfer the unutilized balances to be transferred to the GF of the Municipality; and
require the all concerned personnel to regularly submit liquidation reports to the SAs and to COA, to timely and properly account the utilization of the funds, both in the agency’s records and those of the SAs.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and concerned offices:
Stop the utilization of trust funds which are not in accordance with the specific purpose for which they are created or for which the funds are received, as well the incurrence of expenditure for projects/activities which has no more funds available;
Inform the concerned offices regarding the negative balances of their fund and advise them to effect the immediate restoration of the fund so that these will become available to the funds to which these are borrowed; and
Draw the adjusting entries to correct the balances of the Due to Other LGUs and receivable accounts presented in the financial statements.

Management Comment:
The MDRRM, Treasury and Accounting Offices were grateful of the commendation regarding the proper transfer and accounting of unspent LDRRMF and its proper utilization. They committed the continuous adherence with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Likewise, we recommended the following:
Hasten the reprogramming of the savings from prior years’ implementation so that these will be implemented in the succeeding year and will result in the immediate prevention or mitigation of adverse effects of disasters that may befall in the Municipality;
Implement the planned activities or projects under the continuing appropriation, if these are still warranted, so that the funds will not become idle.  Otherwise, realign the funds to more notable and priority PPAs of MDRRM Office.
Continue to improve the utilization of the MDRRM funds and to comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the inspectorate team to conduct final inspection and to render report as basis for the certification that the project is completed, inspected and accepted for every completed projects before the retention money are released to the contractors after one year from the date of completion.  This certification must be attached to the disbursement vouchers covering the release of the retention money; and
Require the Contractors to submit or post warranty security upon final inspection and acceptance of the  project.

We recommended that the Management instruct the GSO and the BAC Chairman to coordinate regularly to comply with the regulations and furnish the Auditor with the required copies of purchase orders and contracts together with complete supporting documents within five (5) working days from the execution of a contract or issuance of PO for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same, in adherence to COA Circular 2009-001.
We recommended also that the Accountant and the Treasurer ensure that the attached contracts or POs were duly stamped “received” by COA before the payments are processed and disbursed, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-004.  Otherwise, payments may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the primary responsible official of the Municipality, require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all other concerned personnel to submit immediately the unsubmitted financial reports, transaction documents (vouchers and official receipts) for post-audit.  Strict observance of the prescribed submission dates of mandatory reports as required under Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 is highly recommended.
We further recommended that the Management thru the Accounting Office ensure to perform their main official function and responsibilities by producing an organized, complete and accurate financial reports as an end-product of the accounting system, and submit thereof to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management:
Coordinate with GPPB or GPPB-accredited bodies which offer seminars and trainings, to seek schedules of trainings and seminars relative to procurement, and schedule the attendance of the new composition of BAC, within six (6) months after they are designated;
Develop and implement own in-house training programs in accordance with the guidelines and standards prescribed by the GPPB to capacitate also other personnel of the Municipality; and
Ensure that the BAC are capable and capacitated to perform their roles, duties and responsibilities, to achieve Municipality’s governance and operational effectiveness.

For these audit observations, we recommended that the:
Concerned personnel to choose only one vehicle to remain as their service vehicle assigned to them, and to surrender or turn-over the other service vehicles to the Property Custodian or GSO so that these may be issued to other authorized personnel or can be available for common use of the Municipality employees; and
GSO and the accountable officers for these vehicles ensure that the utilization of all motor vehicles are properly requested, approved, documented and recorded for accountability and transparency purposes.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Stop the payment of TAs to the officials who are already assigned with or used government vehicle;
Require the refund of the transportation allowance totaling P216,360.00 granted to concerned employees.  Otherwise, these will be disallowed in audit; and
Instruct the accounting office to ensure that the grant of TAs is aligned with the provisions of various law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management:
Fill the vacancies which are provided with budget with qualified individuals by following the process of recruitment to reduce the list of casuals and job orders that are doing regular agency functions and to reduce also the charges in the maintenance and other operating expenses which could be used for other social services of the LGU. This will boost their morale and they may enjoy the benefits of a regular employee. Without regular hiring, continuous separation of employees due to retirement, dismissal, resignation and death, and new created positions, vacant positions will accumulate which affects the services of the Municipality;
Design policies or procedures that will maximize the services of both the permanent and non-permanent personnel involved in the administrative, clerical and utility works, such as assignment of multi-tasks to personnel (dual functions or serving two or more offices), centralizing personnel that provides janitorial services or centralizing procurement activities to GSO in line with appropriate rules and regulations;
Stop engaging the services of excessive and unnecessary casual, COS and JOs, otherwise, payment of their wages will be disallowed in audit; and
Instruct the HRMO to:
Evaluate the accomplishments of each administrative, clerical, and utility workers to identify duplication or redundant responsibilities, and to immediately recommend the termination of the contracts to avoid incurrence of additional unnecessary expenditures.  At the same time, asses the needs of administrative, clerical and utility of each office and designate a reasonable number of non-permanent personnel to complement the existing permanent personnel to deliver effective and efficient services.  Also, devise an accomplishment report that is quantifiable and specifically detailed and distinctive from the accomplishment of others to ensure that the services being paid are really rendered and earned by them; and
Assess the existing organizational structure of the Municipality, taking into consideration the service requirements and financial capability thereof, to reduce the hiring of casual and COS/JO personnel.

the MA to recognize in the books the RPT online collections totaling ₱326,721.47, and
the three issued checks totaling ₱8,500.00, to ensure a fair presentation of affected
cash accounts.

from their regular duties to complete the physical count of PPE and submit the
RPCPPE to the MA and AT not later than January 31 of each year; (b) require the
GSO to (i) maintain PCs for each type of property in coordination with the MAO;
and (ii) maintain LRIRM and LRNPC in coordination with the Municipal Engineer
(ME); (c) direct the (i) MA to maintain and keep updated the PPELCs; and (ii)
Municipal Engineer to provide the MA and GSO the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects to reconcile their records.

requirements on the granting of CAs and submission of liquidations thereof to ICFAU
as provided by COA–DBM–DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs - DND JC
No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

from various agencies totaling ₱9,352,019.80, in their appropriate accounts to ensure

5.20 We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Municipal Development
Council (MDC) members to ensure that identified PPAs are well-planned and

and enhance Municipal development planning and project prioritization to ensure

Heads to refrain from charging ineligible expenditures against the LDRRMF; (b) MA
and BAC Secretariat to submit explanation or justification on noted documentary
deficiencies; (c) MA to (i) verify the completeness of supporting documents prior to
payment, and (ii) submit the unsubmitted DVs totaling ₱9,747,570.00.

7.20 We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the MENRO Head to (i) encourage
active participation of barangay officials to support the SWMP of the Municipality by
educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or
recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the
implementation of environmental laws; (ii) monitor the barangays without MRFs and
provide technical/financial for them to establish their own MRFs; and (iii) review the
Municipal Ordinance No. 56 and identify the provisions that require strict
implementation and revisions to ensure the attainment of the objectives for enacting
the Ordinance; and (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide a budget for
the rehabilitation of perimeter fence at the Municipality’s MRF.

8.13 We recommended that the (a) LCE require the MT to continuously exert her
best effort to improve tax collection efficiency; and (b) Sangguniang Bayan members
(i) prioritize the enactment of revised MRC in accordance with Section 191, Book II
of RA No. 7160; and (ii) cause appropriate legislative action on the inconsistent

Technical Working Group, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database to facilitate
better and efficient formulation of PPAs to truly address GAD issues and concerned
in the locality.

1.16. We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) update the list of past due RPT payers in order to report reliably the total delinquencies of the Municipality as of report dates; and (ii) employ effective collection strategies by updating and posting the notice of delinquency for property taxes at designated areas and conduct massive campaign and public information dissemination on the value of paying taxes; and if warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254(b) and 256 of RA 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes; and (b) Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT/SET receivables in order to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements.

2.20. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee and GSO to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2023 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the accounting records; and (b) GSO to update PPE records such as Property Cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment and submit IIRUP for the identified unserviceable assets; and (c) Municipal Accountant and GSO to consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to improve the reliability of the PPE accounts.

3.4. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GSO to prioritize the registration of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality with the LTO to prevent possible loss and misuse of the assets and to avoid the incurrence of possible penalties, fines or sanctions from the LTO.

4.5. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the GSO to facilitate the insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality so that the LGU may be indemnified or compensated for any damage or loss due to possible casualties that may happen on these insurable assets.

5.13. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to (i) make proper coordination on timely determination of the completion of infrastructure projects in order to subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the necessary adjusting entries as mentioned above; (ii) compute and record the related Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation reckoning from the date of completion of the projects; and (iii) strictly comply with the recording requirements provided in Section 50, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

6.12. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct verification, analysis and validation in order to determine the details, whereabouts and validity of the unaccounted balances of Due to NGAs account totaling ₱118,368.90; (b) determine the source agencies and the status of programs, projects and activities for which those funds were received and return the unutilized portion as necessary; and (c) maintain complete records of the same.

7.19. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct (a) the Municipal Accountant to (i) perform verification and reconciliation of balances of Due to LGUs account of the Municipality with the Due from LGU-Santa Rosa account of the Provincial Government to establish the correct and accurate balances of both accounts; (ii) trace the pertinent documents to facilitate the liquidation of the completed project costing ₱49,438.00; and (iii) in both cases, draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the corrected/adjusted balances of Due to LGUs and Subsidy from LGUs accounts; and (b) all department heads to require their respective offices to maintain logbooks to record any incoming and outgoing documents which should serve as a tracking tool on the whereabouts of document processing.

8.21. We recommended and the Local Development Council (LDC), headed by the LCE agreed to continue to plan and effectively implement priority developmental projects allowing to flow essential benefits to constituents. We also recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to immediately submit the noted lacking documentary requirements; and (b) BAC to (i) strictly require the compliance with the project requirements during the bidding process in order to ensure the qualification of the contractor’s personnel and the availability of their equipment; and (ii) require the contractors to put up warranty security in order to protect the Municipality from potential costs, or worse, infrastructural failure.

9.19. We reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the LDRRMC, through the LDRRM Officer, to properly and effectively identify LDRRMF related PPAs to be undertaken during the budget year in order to ensure their full implementation and attainment of the expected outcomes within the targeted time frame. We further recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) revert back the unobligated balance of 2017 MF under the Special Trust Fund to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and cause the proper adjusting entry thereof; and (ii) monitor and track the utilization and balances of the Special Trust Fund to ensure the timely reversion of the said fund to the General Fund.

10.25. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) coordination of the BPLO, Acting Municipal Treasurer and Sangguniang Bayan to (i) regularly review and update the Municipal Revenue Code at least every five years, as mandated by law, in order to optimize its revenue generation for the benefit of its constituents; (ii) ensure that the amended Revenue Code reflects the current economic conditions and complies with the Local Government Code of 1991; (b) BPLO to (i) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) ensure that the prescribed rates are correctly applied based on the line of business of the taxpayer; and (iii) require the business tax payers to submit a sworn statement of gross sales/receipts for the initial basis of computation of business tax and a copy of ITR for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (c) Acting Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (d) BPLO and Acting Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate other businesses aside from the 2 noted businesses and demand the payment of computed under collection of business taxes amounting to ₱373,726.14.

11.24. We further reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the OIC-MENRO to study the viability of the implementation of alternative waste disposal methods stated in the Municipality’s 10 -Year SWMP such as reuse and recycling as those will further reduce residual wastes.

12.23. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), through the GAD Focal Person, to (a) continue its efforts to improve gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations; and (b) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the Municipality.

12.24. We further recommended that the GFPS revise its budgeting process to ensure a clear delineation of funds allocated for GAD initiatives and those earmarked for programs serving SC and PWD.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments.
