No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997 in the withholding of premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government's share and its timely remittances to GSIS.

15.4. We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

16.4. We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer continue to comply with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (a) exert efforts and locate all unsubmitted disbursement vouchers; and (b) prepare and submit the Report of Checks Issued pursuant to the requirements of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer (a) closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors; and (b) immediately submit to the Audit Team the documents for the aforementioned transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to (a) conduct a comprehensive physical inventory count covering all the PPEs owned by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; (b) intensify efforts to obtain the needed documents or information for the recognition and valuation of the donated PPEs, otherwise record them in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and (c) coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office for the reconciliation of accounting records with the RPCPPE for the identification of differences in the PPE account.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Designated General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant, after exhaustion of all diligent efforts, to evaluate any PPE discrepancies if the case would qualify for the application of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances for the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE items under COA Circular No. 2020-006, provided that all the requirements specified in Items 7 and 8 are met; (b) department heads to account all donated PPEs under their custody and immediately submit all pertinent documents to the Municipal Accounting Office for the accurate recognition in the books; (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the donated PPEs in the books of accounts; and (ii) make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the PPE account; (d) OIC-Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant t copy of document for the special patent of the parcel of land mentioned above to facilitate the immediate recording in the books; and  (e) Municipal Assessor, along with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, facilitate the registration and titling of the parcels of land, if validated owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Payable account to ensure that only valid obligations of the Municipality are presented in the financial statements; (b) prepare a Schedule of Accounts Payable containing all the necessary information, such as the name and address of the creditor, date the obligation was incurred and aging of the account; and (c) following a thorough review, revert the excess payables and those outstanding for more than two years without substantiated claims, whether administrative or judicial, or which were not covered by perfected contracts or records, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct thorough analysis of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account; (b) make the necessary adjusting entries warranted to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts; and (c) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 pertaining to the reversion of the unexpended balance of DRRMF to avoid the occurrence of the same error in the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of the Local Development Council, direct the members and secretariat of the LDC, along with the implementing departments, to (a) enhance the process of planning, executing, monitoring, and evaluating programs and projects prioritized under the 20% Development Fund to guarantee that the programs and projects are well-prepared and ready for implementation; (b) aim for the timely completion and full implementation of the projects through collective efforts to ensure the prompt realization of benefits for the intended beneficiaries.
Further, we reiterated our recommendation that the Local Development Council ensure that the specific details of projects to be implemented are included in the Annual Budget in accordance with applicable laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRM Officer to (a) monitor and ensure the implementation of the DRRM-related PPAs; (b) prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the prior years’ unexpended balance; and (c) furnish the Audit Team, Office of the Civil Defense with copy of the LDRRMFIP and monthly utilization reports in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all implementing departments to continue monitoring the implementation of programs and projects to ensure the timely implementation/completion thereof.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each account of the General Ledger pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 and the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all implementing departments to continue monitoring the implementation of programs and projects to ensure the timely implementation/completion thereof.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each account of the General Ledger pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 and the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the GFPS, instruct the members, the GAD Technical Working Group (TWG) and GAD Focal Person (a) to comply with the requirements on the institutionalization of GAD mechanisms and mandate of promoting a gender-responsive environment in the local government; and (b) in coordination with Municipal Planning and Development Office, to set up a GAD database and maintain the same to ensure that the identified programs/projects/activities and gender issues/GAD mandate are gender responsive and addresses the needs of the constituents and employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Office,
allot sufficient budget and invest more on facilities, equipment, technologies and processes to enhance proper solid waste management;
devise mechanisms to reduce the volume of garbage and residual wastes being turned-over to Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation (MCWC) to decrease the amount of tipping fees being paid by the Municipality; and
monitor the implementation of laws, rules and regulations in connection with solid waste management for strict enforcement and continuously educate the public through the intensive conduct of continuous forums and seminars to raise awareness and increase the compliance rate of households and residents.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, consider to provide additional manpower to the MENRO to increase compliance with R.A. No. 9003 and to aid in the strict imposition of fines and penalties against violators of prohibited acts.
Moreover, for the disposal of residual solid wastes, we recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality strictly comply with the required procurement process to ensure that that the most advantageous price will be obtained by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (a) strictly observe the provisions of  updated revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 on the process/procedures in the receipt and opening of bids, evaluation of bids, post-qualification of the lowest calculated bid, preparation of contract and issuance of Notice to Proceed and completeness of the documents supporting the procurement activities, contract and disbursement vouchers; and (b) submit a written explanation on the noted inconsistencies with the provisions of the updated revised IRR .
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other municipal offices/units, (a) submit the lacking documents; and (b) henceforth, ensure that all payments to contractors are properly supported with the required documents, in order to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit.
Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to (a) include the cost of personal protective equipment into the detailed estimates for all infrastructure projects; (b) remind the contractors to cause the implementation of the necessary preventive measures such as the provision of PPE and imposition of wearing the same during project implementation to eliminate or minimize the exposure of their workers to hazard; and (c) observe the provisions of the updated revised IRR on the appropriate period of accepting the completed projects to maximize the time that the projects are covered with warranty.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to (a) closely monitor the implementation of all projects; (b) to regularly check the condition of completed projects for early detection of defects; (c) require the Contractor concerned to perform the appropriate repair of the portion of the projects found to be defective at no additional cost to the Municipality; and (d) in coordination with the contractor, provide a detailed As-Built plans for the projects listed in Paragraph  11.9.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned offices to (a) submit copies of abovementioned contracts; and (b) furnish the Audit Team with copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five working days from the issuance of the purchase orders and perfections of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO) to (i) apply the appropriate categories for business establishments; and (ii) levy and collect taxes in compliance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code; (b) Municipal Treasurer's Office (MTO) and other concerned offices to revise the Revenue Code in order to clearly express the intention of the Municipal Government to arrive at higher taxes; (c) BPLO and MTO to require the submission of sworn statement of gross receipts or sales and certified copies of income tax returns so as to come-up with an accurate basis for the computation of business taxes; and (d) BPLO and MTO to verify, assess and validate the noted discrepancy in the computation of business taxes.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accounting Office, Human Resource Management Office, and Municipal Treasurer’s Office conduct monthly reconciliation between the recognized withheld taxes and collected contributions/premiums and loan amortization payments and the remittances made.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office conduct a thorough review and reconcile the outstanding balances of the Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts and recognize the adjusting entries, if necessary, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the (i) Accounting and Treasury Offices agreed to
exercise prudence in the preparation of checks, facilitating fund transfers, recording
and monitoring transactions affecting the cash accounts, and (ii) Accounting Office
ensure the timely submission and completeness of information shown in the BRS
presented in the appropriate format and process the immediate recording of book
reconciling items identified in the BRS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer
to intensify and strategize tax collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of
demand letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places and tapping the
assistance of the barangay officials for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables based on updated
RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card that is sourced from a reliable records to prevent the
inclusion of penalties thereof.

We recommend and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the
Liaison Officer of the Mayor’s Office to timely submit the transmitted liquidation
reports to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books and eventual
submission to the COA ATL.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation and the LCE agreed to a) direct
the Inventory Committee to (i) facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances
through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
and (ii) prepare and submit RPCPPE in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs; b)
instruct the GSO to prepare and maintain property cards; and c) give preferential
attention in registering the seven parcels of land under the name of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
agreed to instruct the a) Officers and employees in charge of the implementation of
projects funded by other agencies to (i) act swiftly in identifying priority projects that are
acceptable to the SAs which are within the parameters of MOA/MOU, taking into
account the time frame of implementation of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of
public service to the intended beneficiaries; and (ii) make representation to the OP for the
immediate action to be taken by the LGU whether to still utilize or return the ₱5,000,000 fund, as well as the unutilized fund amounting to ₱1,030,000.00 from DA; and b) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to practice the prompt liquidation of fund transfers including the immediate refund of unutilized balance of the said funds.

We further recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the officers and
employees in charge of the implementation of projects funded by other agencies to
exert their best efforts in coordinating with SAs to determine whether the purposes
of the fund transfers can still be achieved, and warrant a refund, if necessary.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the
(a) BAC and Accounting Office to submit the lacking supporting documents, as
discussed, and ensure the completeness of documentation before the processing of
payment of claim/s by the contractor/s; and (b) Municipal Planning and Development
Office (MPDO) to comply with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1
specifically in ensuring that that the development projects to be funded out of the 20%
DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation-ready.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the
(a) MDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMFIP in the format as prescribed by COA
Circular No. 2012-002 and (b) BAC and Accounting Office to submit the lacking
supporting documents, as discussed, and ensure the completeness of documentation
before the processing of payment of claim/s by the contractor/s.

We commended Management for the efficient collection performance and
recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to (i) continue to comply and enforce the Local Revenue Code so that
correct business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality and (ii) require the
submission of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of
income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the business establishments are
reliable and accurate; and (iii) examine the books of businesses to ascertain the
correctness of assessment and collection of business tax.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the
members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical
Working Group (TWG) (i) to equip themselves with the HGDG tool and PIMME to
determine how much of the Agency's actual disbursements in implementing its major
programs can be attributed to GAD; and (ii) enhance the GAD AR by completely
filling out the needed qualitative aspect of the activities implemented including data
on sex-aggregation pursuant to MCW.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the MSWMB to (i)
prioritize the update of the SWMP to ensure its sustainability, viability and
effectiveness; (ii) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budgets of the Municipality;
and (iii) oversee the implementation and completion of programmed SWM projects
and interventions to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully achieved.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue withholding and
remitting deductions in accordance with the aforesaid guideline.

However, we further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
provide a separate schedule detailing the discrepancies between the monthly withheld
and remitted amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding of the amounts
posted in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue withholding and
remitting deductions in accordance with statutory regulations.

However, we further recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (i)
Accounting Office and the Human Resource and Management Office to Office to take
the necessary measures to lessen or eliminate the over and under remittances of the
balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, and (ii) Municipal
Accountant to provide a separate schedule detailing the discrepancies between the
monthly withheld and remitted amounts to have a clear and thorough understanding
of the amounts posted in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the IC to immediately complete
the process of one-time cleansing of PPE. It is suggested that the City Mayor
temporarily relieve the members of the Committee from their usual tasks and
obligations, so they could fully focus on the completion of one-time PPE
cleansing.

Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.

We hereby reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive require the:
(a) City Accountant to prepare and submit BRS within the period prescribed
under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Item 3.2 COA Circular No. 96-011,
necessary to substantiate the difference between cash balances per book
and per bank and to effect the necessary adjustments to accounts, so as to
arrive at reliable and correct cash in bank balance;
(b) City Accountant and the City Treasurer to trace the material variance of
P325,326,223.36 between the SLs and Cashbook balances and draw JEV to
effect the necessary adjustment to accounts;
(c) City Accountant and City Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of their
balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected by either of
the offices will be adjusted/taken up in their respective books to ensure the
correctness of the balance reported as CIB-LCCA; and
(d) City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer to provide a written justification
for the repeated non-compliance with the previous year’s audit
recommendation; otherwise, administrative sanctions shall be imposed
pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445.

Considering that this observation is recurring, it is advised that the City
Accountant re-visit and evaluate the current work set-up, necessary to come up
with an acceptable approach to improve the reporting system, which may
include re-assignment of work and/or personnel, hiring of additional workforce,
etc.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to coordinate
with the concerned SAs to address the discrepancies in the Due to NGAs
accounts and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balance of the
accounts is reconciled with the books of the SA.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to reconcile the
Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect adjustments
to reflect the correct balances of the said accounts.

Henceforth, conduct regular/periodic reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds
and Due to Other Funds accounts in observance with the requirement of Section
111(2) of PD No. 1445 so as to ensure that reliable financial information is
provided in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
(a) utilize the reports produced by the existing collection electronic system to
facilitate verification and recording of transactions;
(b) immediately account the 20 percent of the proceeds of permit fees and other
charges as trust liabilities to be disposed as follows:












It is likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer, in
coordination with the City Accountant to remit the long outstanding amount due
of P451,285.67 to the DPWH. Henceforth, regularly remit the five percent share
of DPWH on collected building fees at least on a monthly basis.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit
immediately to the Office of the Auditor the 2,904 paid DVs and their supporting
documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of transactions and to avoid
audit suspension of transactions, which could mature into disallowance.

It is likewise recommended that the City Management revisit the flow of
documents to identify process/es that needs improvement and/or modification.
Circularize any changes in the flow of documents and transactions for general
information.

Henceforth, require the submission of accounts within the period prescribed
under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor to:
(a) require the City Accountant, City Treasurer and City Human Resource
Management Office (HRMO) to provide a written justification or
explanation on the excess amount CA for payroll fund;
(b) instruct the concerned Offices to practice caution in the preparation of
payroll;
(c) instruct the City Accountant to ensure that the amount of CAs for the
payment of salaries, wages, allowances and honoraria are equal to the net
amount of payroll for a pay period; and
(d) formulate additional controls for the early detection of errors and to prevent
occurrence of excessive cash advance.

Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 4.1.1 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to deny
the request for an additional cash advance if the Disbursing Officer (DO)
has an existing unliquidated cash advance.

It is likewise recommended that the City Management consider paying
collective claims of its personnel such as overtime, cell card allowances, such
as overtime services, cell card allowances, honoraria, hazard pay, subsistence
allowance, subsistence and laundry allowance of permanent employees, as
well as wages of personnel under Job Order and Contract of Service thru
Automatic Payroll System (APS) to ensure better control over handling of cash
and administrative efficiency.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
(a) Collecting Officers to record transactions in the cashbook based on actual
dates of collection and remittance and provide information on Unremitted
Collections (Balance on Hand) of the cashbook; and
(b) City Accountant to observe cautiousness in recording transactions by
conducting adequate review of reports from source documents, so as to
record transactions on actual dates and produce accurate records/reports.

Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 25 and
26 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the City provide a specific facility that will offer an
avenue for the submission of proof of payment for ease of reference necessary for
recording of transactions. The City may consider utilizing its website to upload
proof of payment or coordinating with the LBP for possible linkage of electronic
platforms maintained by the bank and the City.

It is likewise recommended that the City Mayor to instruct the:
(a) City Information Technology Office (CITO), in coordination with the City
Treasurer Office (CTO) and the City Accounting Office (CAO), to evaluate the
existing payment option being offered by the City in order to identify issues
and concerns of all the concerned Offices and formulate modification/s on
the current collection options;
(b) CAO to record transactions based on actual date of occurrence and using the
proper account code;
(c) CTO and CAO to exert extra effort in order to identify unrecorded collections
and establish basis for recording collection; and
(d) CAO to draw JEV to effect adjustments to accounts.

Periodic evaluation of all existing system is further recommended in order to
prevent recurrence of similar deficiencies.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the accountable officer to:
(a) submit justification on noted delays in remittance; and
(b) fully remit all her collections daily to City Treasurer to avoid the risk of loss
or misuse of City funds.

Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of Section 69 of PD No. 1445
and Section 7 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer for the automatic
remittance of the collected Fire Code Fees to the Bureau of Treasury to be made
not later than two days after the date of collection in compliance with Section 11
of the CSC - ARTA - DTI JMC No. 2019-001.

We recommended that the City Mayor to instruct the CPDO to prepare medical and
healthcare related programs that shall be funded out of the fund received from
PCSO representing the City’s shares from the STL draws, in compliance with RA
No. 1169, to ensure that benefits therefrom are extended to the intended
beneficiaries.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to
facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of all insurable properties so
that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the
GSIS under the General Service Insurance Fund.

We also recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with the City Budget
Officer, include in the annual budget sufficient appropriation for insurance
expenses to ensure that all insurable properties are covered by property
insurance with the General Service Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Planning
Development Officer to:
(a) properly prepare the proposed AIP, indicating therein the timing of
PPAs execution with consideration on project implementation period;
and
(b) coordinate with the City Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) for proper
scheduling of procurement of PPAs, considering among others
possible conduct of EPA of projects as provided under Section 7.6 of
the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, as amended.

Henceforth, comply strictly observe the requirement of Sections 7.1 and 7.6
of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, as amended.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CDRRMO to:
(a) adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMIP for effective
utilization of LDRRMF; and
(b) facilitate the early preparation/formulation of LDRRMFIP and other related
reports in order that the planned PPAs are immediately implemented and
assured the attainment of the City’s objective of mitigating the impacts of
disaster/calamities towards building a disaster-resilient communities.

Likewise, execute/undertake proper planning to ensure that all PPAs as
provided in the LDRRMFIP are fully executed.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
(a) direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (CDRRMO) to
stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities that are
not related to the DRRM activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC
No. 2013-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; and
(b) refund from the General Fund to the Trust Fund Trust Liability- DRRM the
amount of P2,423,190.00 representing the inappropriate utilization of the
LDRRMF to programs/activities that do not fall under the specifications
provided under the law and statutes and submit to this Office the journal
entry voucher and the disbursement voucher of the said refund, otherwise,
such will be disallowed in audit.

We strongly recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned Disbursing
Officer to submit proof that the amount of P4,484,552.59 were actually claimed
by respective recipients and Official Receipts (ORs) or any equivalent document
showing proof of remittance of excess CAs, otherwise, instruct the City
Accountant to recognize Advances for Payroll for the same amount.

It is likewise recommended that the City Accountant scrutinize documents
supporting liquidation of CAs and to practice caution in recording of
transactions.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to submit:
(a) the lacking documents to establish propriety of claims, otherwise, the same will be
subjected to Notice of Suspension; and
(b) justification on processing of claims despite the absence of complete documents.

Henceforth, comply strictly with the requirement of item 5.10 and 5.14 of the COA
Circular 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
(a) instruct the Human HRD to update and reconcile records so as to establish
beginning balances necessary for the conduct of parallel testing;
(b) create a team that will conduct parallel testing of manually and
electronically maintained leave cards to be composed of representative
from HRD, Information Technology Office (ITO), City Administrative
Department, City Accounting Office and Internal Audit Office; and
(c) instruct the created team to conduct parallel testing of the two systems by:
















































a. running both systems simultaneously, ensuring that both records are
updated and all entries for those records are the same so as to facilitate
comparability;
b. compare the outputs generated by the manual and electronic system
periodically to identify any discrepancies or inconsistencies;
c. investigate discrepancies by identifying the root cause to determine
whether they are due to system errors, data entry issues or other factors;
d. address any identified issued and make necessary adjustments to ensure
that both systems produce consistent and accurate results; and
e. make periodic report to the City Mayor on the updates of the undertaking.

It is likewise recommended that the team ensure the presence of automated
backups before recommending the discontinuance of manual leave card.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Human Resource Department
to maintain the manual Leave Cards properly, while waiting for the establishment of
the reliability of the digitally maintained records, by:
(a) posting entries in the said record using permanent ink; and
(b) updating the record regularly.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer, in coordination
with the City Accountant, to immediately remit the amount of P33,772,964.31 to
the BIR and henceforth, strictly comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular
No. 23-2012 by ensuring that the amount of all taxes withheld are remitted in full
on or before the 10th day of the following month, otherwise, hold the City
Treasurer, City Accountant and other concerned officials for payment of
surcharges and interests, if any.

We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor direct the City
Accountant, together with the concerned department heads/officials, to strictly
comply with the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of RA Nos. 8291, 7875,
9679, and 11199 by ensuring that the amount Due to GSIS, PhilHealth, HDMF,
and SSS for employees’ premium contributions and loan repayments are
remitted to respective agencies in full and within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. The Municipal Treasurer to ensure the submission of Report of Unreleased Checks as of year-end to the MAO to establish the correct balances of receivables/payables accounts reported in the financial statements; and

b. The Municipal Accountant to revert to the Cash and Cash Equivalent accounts the amount of P2,902,510.27 and recognize the corresponding Payables, Inter-Agency Payables and Other Payables accounts which were understated by P2,236,844.72, P653,665.55, and P12,000.00, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary JEV to correct the noted discrepancies/errors in year-end Adjusting Entries and furnish the Audit Team for verification.

Henceforth, strictly adhere to the pro forma entries set forth by MNGAS for LGUs for recording income, collection, and deposit of RPT and SET.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the recognition of the authority of the Municipal Administrator to countersign the checks drawn in settlement of the Municipality’s obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate the necessity of the following:

a. Designating additional DOs to eliminate the granting of multiple CAs to a DO; and
b. Increasing the amount of bond as a result of the increase in the accountability of the DO.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Deny request for additional CA if the DO has an existing unliquidated CA;
b. Install, implement and monitor a sound system of internal control in granting and subsequent liquidation of CAs; and
c. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of CA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairperson of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat to:

a. Ensure that the APPs for CY 2024 and in the subsequent years contain the prescribed contents prescribed under the GPPB Circular No. 07-2015;
b. Prepare and submit the approved PMR within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester following the format prescribed by the GPPB, copy furnish this Office for review and audit; and
Prospectively, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR and GPPB Circular No. 07-2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC – Municipal General Services Officer to strictly comply with Section 5 of RA No. 656 and ensure that properties are insured with the GIF, as administered by the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer to:

a. Fill-up the vacant plantilla positions, giving priority to qualified casual employees and JO workers; and
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and thoroughly assess the organizational structure and staffing pattern of the Municipality, taking into consideration its service requirements and financial capability, subject to the minimum standards and guidelines prescribed by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee and OIC – Municipal General Services Officer to provide a written explanation for the delays in the submission of the perfected contracts, POs and Notices of Delivery.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat and the OIC – Municipal General Services Officer to:

a. Submit copies of Contracts, POs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five working days after its perfection/issuance in compliance with Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001;
b. Furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods and services as required under Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate the timely inspection of deliveries; and
c. Ensure that POs should contain the brand names together with the specifications of the items pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, together with the project focal person, to communicate the noted deficiencies to the systems developer and to immediately correct the same at no additional cost, or otherwise hold responsible the officials who effected the procurement of the new AIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical count of all PPE owned by the Municipality, including those acquired through purchase or donation, as well as those constructed by administration and located at various stations.

Furthermore, we advised the Municipal Mayor to direct the Inventory Committee to:

a. Prepare an updated Report on RPCPPE based on the actual physical count conducted;
b. Create and update property tags and records based on the results of the physical count, and reconcile them with accounting records; and
c. Implement any other necessary procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to:

a. Immediately submit the following to the Office of the Auditor:
   i. A revised LDRRMFIP which includes the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and
   ii. Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or a Presidential Declaration of state of calamity on the release and use of the 30 percent QRF for the aforementioned transactions totaling P717,935.00.

b. Strictly adhere to Section 4.3 of NDRRMC – DBM – DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and ensure that the 30 percent QRF should only be used as a stand-by fund for relief and recovery projects and activities.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements are duly supported with complete documentation pursuant to fundamental principles under Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify the unremitted withholding taxes and immediately remit or effect the necessary adjustment, if warranted.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office for strictly monitoring monthly preparation of withholding tax and due for remittance to ensure that the balance of Due to BIR account (2-02-01-010) is nil after remittance; and
b. Apply the necessary corrective actions on the erroneous taxes withheld on payments for catering services and henceforth, ensure that appropriate withholding taxes, pursuant to Item B(3) of BIR RR No. 23-2012, are withheld from suppliers to avoid imposition of interest and/or surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee (IC) to complete the reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE
account balance to identify the cause of the variance necessary for the
Municipal Accountant to effect adjustment to accounts; and
b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain updated PPELC and
PC, respectively, necessary for reconciliation of records.
It is likewise recommended that the IC utilize the result of reconciliation of reports
and records for PPE in the conduct of one-time-cleansing.
Henceforth, conduct a complete cycle of physical count of PPE items owned by the
Municipal Government of Santa Maria in compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular 80-124 dated January 18, 1980 and Manual on Property Custodianship.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify completed and fully paid projects
costing P11,723,357.32 from CIP to the corresponding PPE accounts by debiting
Infrastructure Assets and Building and Other Structures and crediting CIP -
Infrastructure Assets, and Building and Other Structures.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 50, Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I, in order to reflect the correct balances of CIP and PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
conduct periodic review of subsidiary records for loans payable accounts so that
the adjustments made will be taken up in their respective books to ensure the
correctness of the balance reported as Loans Payable –Domestic accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable
officers to liquidate all cash advances at year-end and comply strictly with item 5.8
of COA Circular No. 97-002, taking in to consideration the requirement of Item 5.9
of the same Circular.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
other concerned officials to exert effort to collect the subject receivables totaling
P3,232,257.60. If these efforts prove futile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission to the COA of the
request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables together with the
necessary documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned
officials to institute measures, including the imposition of sanctions on negligent
officials, to ensure that all receivables of the Municipal are collected and cash
advances are liquidated on due dates.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) grant cash advance for salaries, wages, allowances, honoraria and other similar
payments equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period; and
(b) subject the prepared payroll to review prior to its approval to ascertain the
correctness of the same.
Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 4.2.1, COA Circular 97-002 .
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances within the allowable
period prescribed by COA Circular 97-002, otherwise, impose penalty which may
include withholding of salary.
Henceforth, comply strictly with Items 4.1.3 and 5 of COA Circular 97-002.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official, officer or employee
without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance. It is likewise recommended that
the Municipal Mayor designate additional disbursing officers to limit the cash
advance handled by each DO/AO.
Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section
89 of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Treasurer to increase
the bonding requirements of the Municipal Treasurer, Special Disbursing Officer
and two Other Accountable Officers, pursuant to Bureau of Treasury Circular No.
02-2019 and Treasury Order No. 11-2021.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 101 of PD No. 1445, Section 305(f) of the
RA No. 7160, items 5 and 7.3 of BTr Circular No. 02-2019 and Item 5 of BTr Office
Order No. 11-2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement within the period prescribed under
Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and item 3.2 COA Circular No. 96-011, necessary to
substantiate the difference of P891,739.49 between book and cash book, and
draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment to
accounts; and
b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made
and/or errors detected will be taken up in their respective books to ensure the
correctness of the balance reported as Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current
Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services
Officer (MGSO) to prepare documents necessary for disposal such as (a) properly
filled-up IIRUP, indicating therein the required information, such as appraised value,
mode of disposition, etc., (b) property ledger card duly certified by the Municipal
Accountant and submit to the Audit Team for inspection and review all the
appraised value.
It is likewise recommended that the MGSO conduct a periodic review of PPE
records so as to establish the necessity of disposal.
Henceforth, the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties has to be
conducted in strictly compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Draw Journal Entry Voucher to effect adjustments to accounts;
b. Ensure that the transactions are accurately and completely recorded by
conducting adequate review of reports from source documents to produce
accurate records/reports; and
c. Conduct periodic review and analysis of accounts, which may include
reconciliation of subsidiary records with the general leger.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 109 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Local Finance Committee to provide sufficient appropriation in the
succeeding annual budget to facilitate transfer of title in the name of the
Municipality; and
b. Designate responsible officials who will facilitate in titling the lots or at least
register with the RD available acquisition documents such as DOAS and TD.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 47 (c) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 or the Local
Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of the RRSPM.
It is further recommended that the Municipality maintain a permanent record of land
acquired for future reference.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the five paid Disbursement
Vouchers and their supporting documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of
transactions and to avoid audit suspension of transactions, which could mature
into disallowance.
Henceforth, require submission of accounts within the period prescribed under PD
No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of all insurable
property so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied
with the GSIS under the General Service Insurance Fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal
Budget Officer, include in the annual budget sufficient appropriation for insurance
expenses to ensure that all insurable property are covered by property insurance
with the General Service Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
monitor the proper implementation of programmed activities in accordance with the
plan and budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to remit barangay share from real
property tax within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with
Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160; and to remit provincial shares from real property
and special education taxes immediately, to enable the maximum and timely
utilization of funds by the province and barangays.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the accountable officer to deposit
all collections promptly and intact with the Authorized Government Depository
Bank to avoid the risk of loss or misuse of Municipality’s funds.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of Section 69 of PD No. 1445, and
Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw JEV to effect necessary adjustments to the accounts; and
b. observe caution in recording transactions by conducting adequate review of
reports from source documents to produce accurate records/reports.
Henceforth, record all transactions based on valid documents compliance with
Section 112 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor the MDRRMO:
a. Adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP for effective
utilization of LDRRMF; and
b. Facilitate the early preparation/formulation of LDRRMFIP and other related
reports in order that the planned PPAs are immediately implemented and assure
the attainment of the Municipal’s objective of mitigating the impacts of
disaster/calamities towards building a disaster-resilient community. Likewise,
execute/undertake proper planning to ensure that all PPAs as provided in the
LDRRMFIP are fully executed.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the MDRRMO to stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and
activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided under NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1
described above; and
b. Refund from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability- DRRM the
amount of P523,874.81 representing the inappropriate utilization of the LDRRMF
to programs/activities that do not fall under the specifications provided under
the law and statutes and submit to this Office the journal entry voucher and the
disbursement voucher of the said refund, otherwise, such will be disallowed in
audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning
Development Coordinator, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. Appropriate and utilize the 20 percent DF for PPAs that contribute to the
attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in
accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and
b. Stop the practice of charging regular expenditures against the 20 percent
Development Fund, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed
in audit.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the guidelines of the Joint Memorandum Circular
of the DILG and DBM relative to the utilization of the 20 percent IRA for DFs so that
only legitimate ones are charged against the fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the
COA Team of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit
to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.
Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No.
2012-001 on the processing, approval and payments of claims.

We strongly recommended that Management refrain from resorting to procurement and
disbursements through reimbursement procedure. All payments shall be made
through checks, in compliance with Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular
No. 97-002, so as to enable the government to withhold taxes therefrom.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the
COA Team of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit
to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.
Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001
on the processing, approval and payments of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal General Services
Officer to:
a. Cause the updating of PAR of PPE issued to Municipality’s Officers and
Employees; and
b. Issue renewal of issued PARs every three years or during transfer of
accountability.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirement of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare RCI summarizing therein all issued and cancelled checks.
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions stated to Sections 55 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor all monthly and quarterly
financial reports; and
(b) submit year-end financial statements and schedules to the Office of the Auditor
within the prescribed period.
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1,
Section 41.2 of PD No. 1445 and item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
conduct review and analysis of the Due to GSIS and Due to PhilHealth account in
order to establish the cause of the abnormal balances and to effect the necessary
adjustments noted during the said undertaking.
Henceforth, properly monitor and conduct periodic review and evaluation of
accounts to produce accurate records in compliance with the requirements of
Sections 123 and 124 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to immediately remit the amount of
P1,255,227.37 to the BIR and henceforth, strictly comply with BIR Revenue
Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 by ensuring that the amount of all taxes
withheld are remitted in full on or before the 10th day of the following month,
otherwise, hold the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other
concerned officials for payment of surcharges and interests, if any.

We recommended that the Chairperson on the Inventory Committee, the Municipal Accountant, and other concerned officials conduct reconciliation of PPE records maintained by the Accounting Unit and Property Unit as of December 31, 2023 to determine accountability for lost, damaged, and stolen PPE items and in preparation for the conduct of physical count of PPE and rendering the RPCPPE for CY 2024.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that:
The GSO or the authorized property custodian prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Local Road Networks and maintain the local road inventory, road map and property cards for all roads and their components;
The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of LRN and render the required Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network; and
The Municipal Accountant comply with the required template in the preparation of Local Road Network Ledger Cards.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Audit Team the lacking SSMI for CY 2023, including the stock cards and the RPCI as of December 31, 2023, including a written explanation on the non-submission of such, for further evaluation.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the related ledgers and supplies ledger cards for all inventory and supplies and materials expenses accounts, including a written explanation on the non-submission of such, for further evaluation.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a JEV to adjust the unspent balance of the LDRRMF to exclude the unspent capital outlay of ₱57,600.00.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or field collectors to comply with the following as required in the LTOM, 2nd Edition, Book II, including the instructions in the above-cited Forms and Annexes, on the proper maintenance and updating of cashbooks of all Accountable Officers, such as: (a) updating the cashbook daily; (b) indicating daily running balances in the cashbook; and (c) indicating monthly certification in the cashbook.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the installation of sufficient internal controls over the safeguarding of cash, accountable forms, and other assets in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer through:
Purchase and installation of safety vaults and steel cabinets with vaults where undeposited collections can be safekept;
Purchase and installation of a locked and fireproof steel cabinet or safety vaults where all unissued accountable forms can be safekept; and
Installation of protective mechanisms or barriers on all doors and windows of the Office to prevent unauthorized access.

We recommended that Management:
Initiate steps towards the revision of the approved plantilla of personnel taking into consideration the minimum organizational structure and staffing pattern of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in compliance with Section 472 of RA No. 7160 and Chapter I, Section 5 of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations.
Ensure that the HRMO initiate steps towards the hiring, reassignment and transfer of personnel to the Office of the Municipal Assessor with the end in view of filling in the minimum organizational structure and staffing pattern.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Audit Team copies of the latest tax map and Tax Map Control Roll (TMCR), and all other tax mapping operation records required in the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations, as proof of conduct of tax mapping operations, for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials/ employees expedite the completion of all necessary documents needed for the implementation of all pending development projects as of December 31, 2023 with the end in view of completing all of these projects prior to the end of CY 2024 to the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to submit a written explanation on the utilization of ₱8,469,690.38 without specific sanggunian approval, including submission of additional supporting documents for further evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer, establish controls to ensure that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be determined before year end so that such balance can be transferred to the Trust Fund through the issuance of check within the same calendar year.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team a copy of the disbursement voucher of the transfer of unspent balance to the trust fund for further evaluation.

We recommended that Management:
Initiate steps in filling-in the vacant positions of the LDRRMO in accordance with civil service rules to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management;
Submit an explanation on the noted creation and appointment of the Municipal Government Department Head as head of the LDRRMO contrary to Item 5.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, including proof that the Municipality is financially capable and has not exceeded the PS limitation; and
Submit a written representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to revise the ordinance creating the LDRRMO, taking into consideration revising the salary grades of the LDRRMO personnel in compliance with Item 5.3 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Submit a written explanation on the non-utilization of fund transfers and/or non-implementation of programs/projects/activities totaling ₱6,196,668.85; and
Expedite the utilization of funds received from SAs as of December 31, 2023 totaling ₱6,196,668.85 in accordance with the purpose to which these funds are received, with the end in view of completing all of these projects prior to the end of CY 2024 to the benefit of the constituents.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit a written explanation for the failure to return the unspent balances totaling ₱746,982.34 and cause the return thereof to SAs.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team:
Copies of MOA/Trust Agreement for further evaluation; and
A revised schedule of the Due to LGUs account as of December 31, 2023 fully presenting the noted lacking particulars for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials:
a. 	Revise the Executive Order (EO) on the establishment of the GFPS taking into consideration compliance with Item 4.1(A).2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, with the end in view of making the GFPS well-represented among all sectors in their jurisdiction; and
b. 	Submit to the Audit Team a copy of: (a) latest GAD Database; (b) GAD Code; and (c) an EO establishing the M&E System, if any, including a written explanation for the non-submission of such, for further evaluation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Confer with the PCW, the DILG, or other government authorities on the availability of capability trainings as required in Item 4.1(A)(3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended;
Appropriate sufficient funds for these capability-building programs; and
Require all elected officials and department heads to attend all of the required capability-building programs with the end in view of applying such capabilities towards the effective discharge and implementation of the Magna Carta of Women.

We recommended that the BAC issue written notices within a reasonable period to the BAC Secretariat before each stage for the procurement of goods, infrastructure projects, or consultancy services to ensure that invitation letters were received by the required observers at least five calendar days prior to the earliest scheduled procurement activity.

We recommended that the BAC issue periodic written notices within a reasonable period before each of the deadlines of submission to the BAC Secretariat to ensure compliance by the latter with GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Identify the debtors of these receivable balances for proper issuance of demand to collect such with the end in view of closing these balances and prevent them from being dormant;
Identify the valid claimants of these payables for the proper request for documents to substantiate their claims with the end in view of closing these balances through settlement thereof or reversion to the unappropriated surplus, as the case may be; and
Submit a written explanation on the non-settlement of Accounts Payable totaling ₱835,074.88 and initiate steps to close these balances through settlement thereof or reversion to the unappropriated surplus, as the case may be.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Submit the RPCPPE and RPCI for CY 2023 to the Audit Team once the physical counts are completed as well as the updated property and stock cards for evaluation;
Investigate the causes of the delayed conduct of the physical counts and address accordingly the issues to speed up the process and be able to submit the reports reconciled with the accounting records within the prescribed period; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Submit the updated PPE and Supplies Ledger Cards.

We recommended and the Acting-Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Prepare the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers for CY 2023 and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts and make adjustment, if necessary.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Trace the nature of the transactions made as adjustments on the RPT receivables to determine the proper entries to reconcile the accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer along with the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace the causes of the variances of the two books of accounts so that corrective actions/measures can be undertaken to reconcile the balances of the respective records.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit a written explanation supported with legal basis on the granting of additional RATA to the Acting Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials/employees to deduct retention money or require a special bank guarantee in the procurement of goods, otherwise any expenses that may be incurred due to the defects within the warranty period will be charged to the personal account of the concerned approving officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit a written explanation supported with legal basis on the inclusion of the hotel/lodging in granting per diem on the day of departure to the permanent official station to preclude issuance of a notice of disallowance.
The Municipal Accountant explained in writing that reimbursements for travelling expenses of the Acting-Municipal Treasurer and auditors from the Province of Cebu Treasurer’s Office are given full on the last day because they stayed overnight to finish the task/fieldwork before going back to their official station and leave early on the morning of the following day. However, these are not shown in the itinerary of travels prepared by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office staff and signed by Accountable Officers and approved by Municipal Mayor.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit proof that the said accountable officials stayed overnight in the exigency of service.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and/or other concerned officials make representation with the service provider for the conduct of training to orient the Municipal Budget Officer and other employees in the Budget office on the operation of the E-budget system so that it will be fully utilized and be able to provide efficient services to its clientele and other stakeholders.  Otherwise, the engagement with the service provider be temporarily discontinued until such time that the MBO and other employees are capable and able to use the system.

We recommended and the:
Municipal Treasurer agreed to inquire with the depository bank the details of the debit memo in January 2023 amounting to ₱33,431.97, and subsequently furnish the Municipal Accountant with the documents to record the appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts; and
Municipal Accountant agreed to record all valid and duly supported reconciling items in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) as supporting documents of the recorded expenses as well as record subsequent purchases of inventories in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We recommended that Management expedite the hiring or promotion of qualified personnel to enhance the services and attain full implementation of the Municipality’s programs, projects and activities for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal opportunities to the deserving individuals in the community.

We recommended and the:
LDRRMO agreed to identify the allotment for MOOE and CO on each PPA in the LDRRMFIP of 2023;
Municipal Budget Officer agreed to record the appropriation for MOOE and CO in the RAAO to be able to determine the correct unspent/unobligated amount of each expense class as of year-end; and
Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare adjustments in the books of accounts and/or deposit back to the general fund of any excess due to the inclusion of CO in the transfer to STF of unexpended balances of LDRRMF, if applicable, after the correct amount of unspent DRRM-MOOE is determined.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace the causes of the variances of the accounts to be able to make necessary actions such as immediate remittance of accounts, refund to the employees/personnel or adjustments in the books of accounts, whichever is applicable.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory
Committee agreed to complete the reports necessary to establish the correct
balances of PPE items that should be reported in the financial statements.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to file a request for
authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs to the audit team, and
support its request with the certified lists required in Section 8.3 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006, to take full advantage of the one-time cleansing
privilege afforded by the Commission and establish accuracy of its PPE
balances reported in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to cause the annual
determination of loan amortization due in the next 12 months from the
Statement of Financial Position date and reclassify it under the current
portion of the Financial Liabilities, pursuant to Par. 80 of IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revise the Notes
to the Financial Statements to include the principal amount, interest rate,
maturity date, and interest expense of the Loans Payable – Domestic account,
to properly inform the users of the financial statements, the financial
performance of the Municipal Government, particularly in financial
management.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to, henceforth:
4.8.1 Require the outgoing AO to submit their cashbook to the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant for verification and recording
purposes;
4.8.2 Instruct the newly appointed or designated AO to use a new cashbook
in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
4.8.3 Brief the newly appointed or designated AOs on the proper recording
of transactions and other matters relating to their role as a disbursing
officer.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of assigning
the Cashier to act as a treasurer for other associations or as a collector of
funds for purposes other than his role as a cashier in the Municipal
Government.

We further recommended and the Cashier agreed to refrain from keeping
any other collections in the vault, except for the funds intended for the
government.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from granting cash
advances to an accountable officer on behalf of another, as per COA Circular
No. 97-002.

We further recommended that Management properly assign the disbursing
officer who handles the PCF as the Petty Cash Custodian.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) limit the
procurement through Shopping only for cases that do not exceed the
thresholds prescribed in Annex H of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to use Shopping as the mode of
procurement only when it satisfies the requirements of Section 52.1 of the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We also recommended and the BAC agreed, when recommending approval for
alternative modes of procurement, to indicate the specific
reason/justification for using such mode for each purchase, instead of
providing a uniform justification for all purchases made which did not
undergo competitive bidding.

We recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan agreed to appropriate funds
that will enable Management to conduct a modest yet meaningful celebration
of its various socio-cultural activities by regulating and limiting Christmas
celebration expenditures only to those that are official, relevant, and
necessary to the occasion and henceforth observe the principle of prudent
use of scarce financial resources of the government as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to, henceforth secure the appropriate
authority to purchase motor vehicles in compliance with DBM BC No. 2022-
1 and DILG MC No. 2022-105.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) and the BAC
Chairman, henceforth, use the latest DPRI as the ceiling price in the
procurement of drugs and medicines in compliance with RA 9502 and DOH
AO No. 2019-0040 dated October 14, 2019.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to ensure the indication of complete
detailed specifications in the PO before proceeding with the delivery of drugs
and medicines.

We also recommended and the personnel concerned or the end-user duly
authorized by the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the Procuring Entity, agreed
to fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B
of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the
Sanggunian, agreed to:
13.8.1 Include in its annual budget the appropriation for the establishment of
IAS; and
13.8.2 Create an IAS in the Municipality in accordance with Administrative
Order No. 70 and RA No. 3456, as amended.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the IAS use the 2020
Revised PGIAM in the exercise of their internal audit functions.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, agreed to evaluate the
feasibility and closely monitor the implementation of the various PPAs in
accordance with the approved development plan as required under Section
476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160.

In addition, we recommended and Management agreed to consider all
possible limitations, including manpower and equipment availability, during
the planning stage.

We recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to:
15.15.1 Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor in the preparation of the
updated SMV of the Municipality pursuant to Section 212 of R.A.
No. 7160; and
15.15.2 Initiate the immediate conduct of a general revision of real property
assessments after the enactment of the updated SMVs and ensure
that this is done every three years as required under Section 219 of
RA No. 7160 and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2010-001.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to boost tax
collection in its campaign program to enhance the collection of RPT/SET
Receivables and enforce remedies through the issuance of a warrant on or
before, or simultaneously with, the institution of the civil action for the
collection of delinquent taxes.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) agreed to:
20.8.1 Ensure that budget allocation for GAD is optimally utilized in providing
adequate facilities and services, to address gender issues and the GAD
mandate of the LGU;
20.8.2 Prepare a more realistic and attainable GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to
ensure that GAD projects and activities will be carried out in accordance
with the approved plan; and
20.8.3 Establish an effective monitoring and evaluation system to monitor the
implementation of the annual GPB.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences noted in the Cash in Bank balance between the balances per FS and BRS to present accurate Cash in Bank balance and support fair presentation of the same in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the differences noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and MF-MOOE from the current year appropriation to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability – DRRM” in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-2002 dated September 12, 2012 to avoid possible misappropriation of fund in addressing the disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LGU in the next five years.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance was transferred to Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that no government funds shall be used to fuel privately owned vehicles otherwise, disbursements not consistent with existing law, rules and regulations will be subjected to issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: No similar transaction during the year was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management authorize the grant of Anniversary Bonus to its government officials and employees based on the existing laws, rules and regulations. Any payment not in accordance with the provisions of AO No. 263 and LBC No. 65 shall be refunded by the official or employee concerned.
observations.managementAction: No similar transaction made during the year were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to follow the inventory accounting system/process including the forms to be used and the reports to be prepared for the inventory of goods to present the actual status of Inventory of Supplies held for Consumption and require the immediate issuance of the supplies to the supposed end-user so that the latter would avail of the advantages from using the said supplies. Also, avoid overstocking of perishable and time-sensitive goods to prevent possible wastage of funds from maintaining expired or obsolete products.
observations.managementAction: All inventories for distribution were distributed during the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned officials and employees of the Municipality to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and ensure that the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances, as provided in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, are strictly observed to avoid the sanctions provided by law.
observations.managementAction: The officials and employees concerned liquidated their outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to submit the Inventory Report using the prescribed form of Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment showing relevant information, such as property number, unit value, balance of PPE per property cards and per count pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and undertake all other procedures prescribed under the same Circular to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has not undertaken all procedures pertinent to the one-time cleansing of its PPE pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. Hence, the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment was not accomplished and submitted.
observations.reason: The Management had a difficulty in identifying the accountable persons for missing PPE items.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries for the reclassification of PPE items below the capitalization threshold of P15,000.00 to appropriate accounts; and

Instruct the GSO-Designate to cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of the properties below the capitalization threshold and issue an Inventory Custodian Slip in lieu thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the PPE items below the capitalization threshold of P15,000.00.
observations.reason: The GSO was not able to prepare and issue ICS for properties below the capitalization threshold.
