observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed, through its MDRRMO, to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

Further, we have recommended that the Management refrain from disbursing the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years without the proper approval of the revised AIP or LDRRMFIP from the Local Sanggunian and the LCE.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:

The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible based on updated Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting up the Receivables from RPT and SET;

The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the RPT/SET Receivables established at the beginning of the year based on the submitted duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers to monitor tax collections and delinquencies; and 
The Municipal Assessor to undertake thorough review on the assessments of real properties within the Municipality for taxation so that the Accounting Department could take up a more realistic RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management was only able to implement the audit recommendation at the beginning of CY 2024.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer maintained an individual taxpayer’s index card, however, it was only updated in CY 2023. She was not able to submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (i) Accounting and Treasury Offices continue with the proper accounting of the Cash accounts and monitoring of the book and bank reconciling items; and (ii) Accounting Office religiously prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statement.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of DF so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented.

We also reiterated that the LCE instruct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and its Secretariat to fully comply with the provision of the Revised IRR of the R.A. No. 9184 on the (i) posting of Contracts and NTPs; (ii) inviting observers during bid evaluations and post-qualification activities; and (iii) sending of notifications of bidding results to losing bidders; 

(b) BAC and TWG to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion; and (c) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

Lastly, we recommended that BAC Secretariat and concerned officials submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five days from the execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS and Focal Person to: 

(i) strive for the full implementation of the identified and approved PPAs by utilizing judiciously the fund set aside for said purposes; and 

(ii) set measurable targets and disclose quantifiable actual results in accomplishing the GPB and GAD AR including data on sex-aggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary.
We recommended that Management continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Office (CGSO) to:
a. expedite the registration and/or renewal of registration of MVs with the LTO to avoid impounding, payment of fines and non-indemnification in case of accident or loss; and
b. facilitate the transfer of titles of MVs in the name of the City in order to establish absolute ownership and control over the vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Exerted effort to gather documents necessary to establish absolute ownership were registered with the LTO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to:
 exert extra effort in the collection of market rental fees and penalties to prevent loss from the uncollectible receivables; and
 strictly observe the pertinent provisions in the City Revenue Code on the regular payment of monthly rentals and sanctions for late payment.
observations.managementAction: Exerted extra effort in the collection of market rental fees to market rental fees exerted by the concerned personnel was mainly affected by the on-going renovation of public market. Cancellation of lease contract/agreement for delinquent and non-compliant stallholders were imposed by the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CSWDO to review and undertake appropriate action to refund the unqualified beneficiaries and to ensure strict compliance with DSWD MC No. 009, series of 2020 in the implementation of ESP.
observations.managementAction: The City Management agreed with the audit recommendation. In a letter dated March 20, 2024, the City Accountant commented that assistance of project coordinators from concerned Departments was requested to reconcile the City records with the record of SAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City personnel in-charge to facilitate the transfer of titles of the acquired lands. Also, prioritize lands with pending legal cases in the court to protect the interest of the City.
observations.managementAction: Out of ten untitled lots acquired by the City as of December 31, 2023, two titles acquired by the City were registered with the LTO. The remaining eight are under process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
A. City Accountant to review and analyze corresponding balances to determine the proper disposition of dormant trust funds; and
B. City Accountant and City Treasurer to cause immediate remittance to the National Treasury or the Source Agency fund balances where the purpose for its creation had met/completed, pursuant to Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: All funds under due to NGAs accounts are all moving. Due to NGAs accounts are remitted to the source agencies, details as follows: Bureau of Treasury – Trust receipts from Provincial Treasurer – trust receipts for cash prizes of Laguna City for Anilag Festival amounting P33,044.88, Check No. 61553187 dated Feb. 17, 2023. It was likewise recommended Trust receipts from the Provincial Treasurer’s Office as the moving account. There is an on-going PHIC analysis of the account. Due to LGUs and Due to GOCCs accounts were reclassified to appropriate accounts. As an update, there is an on-going PHIC analysis of the account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
A. City Accountant and City General Services Officer (CGSO) to: 
     maintain a complete and up-to-date Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) and Stock Cards (SCs), respectively, for each inventory items; 
     conduct periodic reconciliation of records maintained by respective offices to ensure that the balances reported are correct and accurate; and 
B. CGSO to: 
     conduct a complete physical count of all inventories on semestral basis and prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) thereon, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The City General Services Office maintained Stock Cards (SC) for inventories based on previous year’s audit recommendation as the one is being directly delivered to end-users/requesting units due to inadequate warehouse space. No Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) is being maintained by the City Accountant. CGSO conducted physical count of all inventories at year-end. Result of the undertaking was furnished to City Accounting Office for reconciliation purpose.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: 
 a. City Accountant and City General Services Officer to: 
    - maintain complete and up-to-date Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs), in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; 
    - conduct periodic reconciliation of records maintained by respective offices; 
 b. City General Services Officer, being the Head of Inventory Team, to: 
    - exclude semi/non-expendable items in the RPCPPE; and 
 c. City Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to derecognize the disposed scrap/unserviceable PPE items in the books of accounts based on the pertinent records received from the CGSO. Henceforth, comply with the provisions.
observations.managementAction: Coordinates with the system provider for possible upgrading of system to address the noted deficiency. Maintenance of PPELCs & PCs will be considered in the upgrading of the software. Adjustments were effected as of December 31, 2023. Appraisal of roads was conducted in CY 2023 in relation to the conducted one-time cleansing. The IC is still in the process of identifying structures to be turned-over to other government entities.
observations.reason: The conducted one-time cleansing of PPE did not allow the Inventory Team to identify the assets presented in lump amount, described as “Balance from CY 2002 Account Conversion/Amount for Reconciliation and Adjustment”. Those assets were reflected in City Accounting Record since 2002. A request for authority to derecognize the afore-cited PPE is to be filed with COA Regional Office No. IV-A, Quezon City.

observations.managementAction: For preparation of RPCPPE and reconciliation of record and report for PPE was utilized in conducted one-time cleansing. The City measured the residual value of all items of property, plant and equipment. The CAO and CEO conducted reconciliation of records necessary to identify possible adjustments. The City Engineer prepared documents to identify infrastructure assets and verifying documents necessary for the de-recognition and recognition of cost in the City’s book of accounts. Verified available documents of projects and updated records.
observations.reason: Result derived from reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE account balance was conducted. However, the said undertaking was not completed, specifically for PPE items that are infrastructure in nature (excluding Road Networks) between the RPCPPE and City Accounting record.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to deny the request for an additional cash advance if the Disbursing Officer (DO) has an existing unliquidated cash advance and ensure that the cash advances granted to the AOs/DOs do not exceed their maximum cash accountabilities. Otherwise, we recommended the increase in the application of fidelity bonds to sufficiently cover their cash accountabilities. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 on the granting of cash advance.
It is likewise recommended that the City Management consider shifting to Automated Payroll System (APS) for the processing and payment of benefits of officials and employees, as well as wages of personnel under Job Order and Contract of Service, to ensure better control over handling of cash and administrative efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The City Management adhered to partial measures: coordinated with LBP for the opening of bank accounts of personnel under Job Order intended for the payment of wages of Job Order personnel. Partial adoption of Automated Payroll System as of December 31, 2023 for the payment of wages of Job Order personnel. Some JOs were not able to open bank account with LBP since other documents are lacking, such as Tax Identification Number (TIN).
observations.reason: Not all Job Order (JO) personnel were able to open bank accounts with LBP since other documents are lacking, such as Tax Identification Number (TIN). Also, inadequate review of payroll hindered the full implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of the City to include stipulations in all procurement contracts requiring contracted suppliers/contractors to regularly present, within the duration of the contract, (a) a tax clearance and (b) a copy of income and business tax returns duly stamped and received by the BIR and duly validated with the tax payments, as required under Section 3 of Executive Order (EO) 398, series of 2005.
observations.managementAction: The BAC requires submission of Tax Clearance Certificate (TCC) by prospective suppliers in the procurement of goods and services involving significant amount. Stipulation on the responsibility of the contractor on the submission and regular updating of Tax Clearance Certificate was incorporated in infrastructure contracts in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, together with the concerned department heads/officials, to strictly comply with the IRR of RA Nos. 8291, 7875, 9679, and 11199 by ensuring that the amount Due to GSIS, PhilHealth, HDMF and SSS for employees’ premium contributions and loan repayments are remitted to respective agencies in full and within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant commented that the audit observations and recommendations are well noted. It was informed that a personnel was already assigned who will specifically work on the statement analysis of the subject accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Inventory Committee (IC) to immediately complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE and PPE account balance to identify the cause of variance and effect the necessary adjustment to accounts. It is likewise recommended that the IC utilize the result of such undertaking in the conduct of one-time cleansing.
observations.managementAction: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the RPCPPE to account balance and the Inventory Committee (IC) to complete the reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE account balance based on the result of the conducted one-time cleansing. A supplemental RPCPPE was subsequently submitted by the IC on March 18, 2024.
observations.reason: The result derived from reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE account balance was conducted. However, partial conduct of one-time cleansing occurred; incomplete reconciliation for infrastructure assets not including Road Networks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to: (a) coordinate with concerned contractors for the submission of copies of Official Receipt (OR) issued by Provincial Treasurer as proof of payment for sand and gravel fees; (b) ensure that the City Accountant conduct confirmation of posted warranty prior to the grant of advances and issue certification to document such confirmation to validate the authenticity of posted warranty. We likewise recommended the City Mayor require the City to ensure that paid payrolls for Job Order (JO) and Contract of Service (COS) are supported with duly verified Accomplishment Reports and/or Daily Time Records (DTRs).
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant coordinated with contractors and requested attached clearance from Provincial Treasurer. Copy of Certification of payment for sand and gravel fees was attached to final payment claims. Duly verified accomplishment reports of concerned JO and COS workers were submitted to the Audit Team. Payments were verified with evidence.
observations.reason: Not all recommendations were implemented. Some payments were not supported with warranty security or full verification. Also, some payroll services for JO/COS were not fully supported with verified accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive require: a. the City Assessor to prepare an updated SFMVs as basis for the conduct of a general revision of real property assessments; and b. the Presiding Officer/Members of Sangguniang Panlungsod calendar, deliberate and cause passage of an Ordinance incorporating the updated SFMVs prepared by the City Assessor and to appropriate funds for the conduct of the general revision of Real Property Assessments.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor prepared an updated SFMVs. The Presiding Officer/Members of Sangguniang Panlungsod deliberated and passed an Ordinance incorporating the updated SFMVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Acting City Treasurer to deposit all collections promptly and intact with the Authorized Government Depository Bank to avoid the risk of loss or misuse of City funds and to strictly comply with the requirements of Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer informed that collections during exceptional periods were kept in Offices’ vault until subsequent deposit. The City exerted efforts to lessen delays in depositing collections during CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require accountable officers for the immediate and full liquidation of respective cash advances; that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to deny request for additional cash advance if the Disbursing Officer has existing unliquidated cash advance; and that the City Mayor require the City Accountant, City Treasurer and City HRMO to provide a written justification or explanation on the excess amount CA for payroll fund; instruct concerned Offices to practice caution in preparation of payroll; ensure CA equals net payroll; and formulate additional controls for early detection of errors.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred. Many outstanding advances were liquidated as of January 31, 2024. The City Accountant communicated issues to Department Heads. Long outstanding cash advance of P33,750.00 will be subjected to a request for write-off. Partial adoption of APS to reduce CA usage.
observations.reason: Non-compliance persists for some items; full adoption of APS and adequate controls not fully implemented; some long outstanding advances still subject to write-off request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to utilize the reports produced by the existing collection electronic system to facilitate verification and recording of transactions; immediately account the 20 percent of the proceeds of permit fees and other charges as trust liabilities (15 percent OBO and 5 percent DPWH); and instruct the City Treasurer, in coordination with the City Accountant to remit the long outstanding amount due to the DPWH. Henceforth, regularly remit the five percent share of DPWH at least on a monthly basis.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant concurred with the recommendations and assured full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, together with the concerned department heads/officials, to strictly comply with the IRR of RA Nos. 8291, 7875, 9679, and 11199 by ensuring that the amount Due to GSIS, PhilHealth, HDMF and SSS for employees’ premium contributions and loan repayments are remitted to respective agencies in full and within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant informed that a personnel was assigned to work on statement analysis of subject accounts. Subsequent remittances were made in January 2024 partially covering the outstanding balances.
observations.reason: Balances remain as of year-end; partial remittances in succeeding year; delays due to missing employee IDs/numbers and records reconciliation needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Assessor prepare updated SFMVs and the Sanggunian deliberate and pass an ordinance incorporating updated SFMVs and appropriate funds for general revision.
observations.managementAction: Prepared an updated SFMVs and Ordinance. The updated SFMVs were incorporated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to ensure timely submission of DVs and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor; and to assess if additional workforce is necessary to address the deficiency.
observations.managementAction: Management reported that as of April 19, 2024, out of the reported 2,094 unsubmitted vouchers, 1,844 DVs amounting to P1,610,749,707.63 were submitted for post-audit leaving 250 DVs totaling P85,647,462.56. The City Treasurer claimed checks issued in last quarter could not be immediately claimed by payees.
observations.reason: Delayed submission persists for some vouchers; City Treasurer justification not accepted by Audit Team; process review recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CDRRMO to: (a) adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMIP for effective utilization of LDRRMF; and (b) facilitate the early preparation/formulation of LDRRMFIP and other related reports so that planned PPAs are immediately implemented.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed. Considering procurement delays, conduct of Early Procurement Activities (EPA) will be considered in ensuing period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: (a) direct the CDRRMO to stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities that are not related to DRRM activities; and (b) refund from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability- DRRM the amount of P2,423,190.00 representing the inappropriate utilization of the LDRRMF to programs/activities that do not fall under the specifications provided under the law and statutes and submit to this Office the journal entry voucher and the disbursement voucher of the said refund, otherwise, such will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Vouchers were drawn on February 29, 2024 to set-up Due to and Due from Other Fund for the refund.
observations.reason: No actual refund was made prior; journal entries set-up in 2024 to reflect refund; full implementation will be monitored.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned Disbursing Officer to submit proof that the amount of P4,484,552.59 were actually claimed by respective recipients and Official Receipts (ORs) or any equivalent document showing proof of remittance of excess CAs, otherwise, instruct the City Accountant to recognize Advances for Payroll for the same amount. It is likewise recommended that the City Accountant scrutinize documents supporting liquidation of CAs and to practice caution in recording of transactions.
observations.managementAction: City Management committed to submit documents that will show proof that the amount of P4,484,552.59 were actually claimed by respective recipients and ORs or any equivalent document showing proof of remittance of excess cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to submit: (a) the lacking documents to establish propriety of claims, otherwise, the same will be subjected to Notice of Suspension; and (b) justification on processing of claims despite the absence of complete documents. Henceforth, comply strictly with the requirement of item 5.10 and 5.14 of the COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred. As of this writing, lacking documents for monetization of leave credits were already submitted. It was assured that lacking documents for overtime services paid will be provided to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Evaluation disclosed that the submitted documents are inadequate. Thus, implementation of the audit recommendation will be monitored until fully complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: (a) instruct the Human Resource Department (HRD) to update and reconcile records so as to establish beginning balances necessary for the conduct of parallel testing; (b) create a team that will conduct parallel testing of manually and electronically maintained leave cards to be composed of representative from HRD, Information Technology Office (ITO), City Administrative Department, City Accounting Office and Internal Audit Office; and (c) instruct the created team to conduct parallel testing of the two systems by running both systems simultaneously, comparing outputs, investigating discrepancies, addressing issues, making necessary adjustments and making periodic report to the City Mayor.
observations.managementAction: The City Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) concurred with the audit recommendations. It was informed that their Office, together with the service provider is currently conducting parallel testing on the HRIS and they will continue to do until reconciliation is achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the HRD to maintain the manual Leave Cards properly, while waiting for the establishment of the reliability of the digitally maintained records, by: (a) posting entries in the said record using permanent ink; and (b) updating the record regularly.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel in the Leave Administration Section is reminded on using permanent black ink in all manual leave cards. They were likewise instructed to update their records regularly and regularly reconcile manually and digitally produced records. In addition, City HRMO requested for a computer software upgrade to address the current and future needs of all offices concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer, in coordination with the City Accountant, to immediately remit the amount of P33,772,964.31 to the BIR and henceforth, strictly comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 by ensuring that the amount of all taxes withheld are remitted in full on or before the 10th day of the following month, otherwise, hold the City Treasurer, City Accountant and other concerned officials for payment of surcharges and interests, if any.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant informed that they are currently conducting analysis of accounts in order to verify and identify cause of the discrepancy between the tax withheld and taxes remitted. Subsequent remittances were made in January 2024 partially covering the outstanding balance.
observations.reason: Balance remains as of January 2024; partial remittances made; P13,718,506.88 unaccounted due to lack of details such as missing TINs of newly hired personnel.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, together with the concerned department heads/officials, to strictly comply with the IRR of RA Nos. 8291, 7875, 9679, and 11199 by ensuring that the amount Due to GSIS, PhilHealth, HDMF, and SSS for employees’ premium contributions and loan repayments are remitted to respective agencies in full and within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant commented that the audit observations and recommendations are well noted. It was informed that a personnel was already assigned who will specifically work on the statement analysis of the subject accounts.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2023, balances in Due to GSIS, PhilHealth, HDMF/Pag-IBIG and SSS existed and were only partially remitted in January 2024 leaving a balance of overdue amount. Reasons include absence of GSIS/Pag-IBIG/PhilHealth numbers for newly hired employees and salary adjustments not yet reflected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the revised SCBAA discrepancies totaling P27,951,335.92 noted between the SAAOB and the SCBAA to arrive at a more accurate and reliable presented in the year-end financial statements; 
b. Municipal Accountant to provide accurate reconciliation of the SCBAA to the Statement of Financial Performance, and to include in the NTFS complete and relevant disclosures pertaining to the budget information of the Municipality; and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare Pre-Closing and Post-Closing Trial Balance supported by SAAOB, respectively, which should be reconciled at the end of each month, pursuant to the requirements of Section 10, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The Management, through the Municipal Accountant, submitted a revised SCBAA as compliance with the audit recommendation.
For Calendar Year (CY) 2023, the Municipal Accountant provided reconciliation of the SCBAA to the Statement of Financial Performance and included in the NTFS.
The submitted monthly Trial Balances were not supported with the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB) and other related schedules.
Discussed in AOM No. 24-002 dated January 18, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers, indicating therein the property classification, and its corresponding collectible amount for the year.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
⮚ To set-up the RPT/SET Receivable based on the duly certified list furnished by the MTO; 
⮚ To draw the necessary JEV to correct the noted errors in the recording of the receipt of payments for RPT/SET; 
⮚ To maintain SLs containing the details or breakdown of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts; and 
⮚ To trace the material discrepancy amounting P57,777,583.22 in the reported RPT and SET Receivable accounts and the Certified List of all Real Property Tax Delinquencies prepared by the MTO; and 
To reconcile the discrepancy noted between the RCD rendered by the MTO and the CRJ prepared by the MAO.
observations.managementAction:
Based on the certified list furnished by the MTO, the MAO drew JEVs to set-up of RPT/SET Receivable.
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the discrepancy between the RCD rendered by the MTO and the CRJ prepared by the MAO.
The MTO and MAO conducted reconciliation of records to address the discrepancy between the RCD and CRJ, respectively.
As a result, the Municipal Accountant drew JEV Nos. 100-2023-05-0039 and 200-2023-05-0010 dated May 26, 2023 to record the delinquencies to RPT/SET Receivables amounting P28,888,791.61, correspondingly.
The MTO and MAO conducted reconciliation of records to address the discrepancy between the RCD and CRJ, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Municipal Accountant and MGSO to reconcile the discrepancies noted between the inventory report and accounting records, and effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at reconciled balances; and
b. Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office to maintain a complete and updated PPELCs and property cards, respectively, pursuant to the requirements of Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to substantiate the reported balance in the RPCPPE and Financial Statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) and Municipal General Services Office (MGSO) undertook reconciliation of its respective records to address the discrepancies noted.
The imposition of the general revision of real property assessment is on a provincial level, the Municipal Assessor can only continuously coordinate with its Provincial counterpart to fast track the revisions on the said assessment.
The Management, represented by the MGSO, assured the Audit Team of the establishment of a new Inventory Committee. This committee will be responsible for conducting physical counts, updating the RPCPPE, affixing property tags, and maintaining accurate GSO records.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not conduct physical count of its properties during Calendar Year (CY) 2023. As a result, the PPELCs and PCs maintained by both MAO and MGSO were not updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Draw the adjusting Journal Entry Voucher to correct the error in the recording of the advance payment for RPT/SET; and
b. Properly present the reclassification of the affected accounts as prior period error or adjustment in the year-end financial statements by restating the balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Based on the certified list furnished by the MTO, the MAO drew JEVs to set-up of RPT/SET Receivable.
The erroneous entry was corrected through the drawing of the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher.
The Management did not provide a reason for its non-compliance with the audit recommendation.
Discussed in observation no. 3, page 29, part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the discrepancy noted between the reciprocal accounts between the Municipality to that of the PGL and effect adjustments through drawing of adjusting Journal Entry Voucher, if warranted; and
b. Henceforth, closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that the account balances will provide a zero effect upon consolidation of all the funds.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant requested details of the Due to LGU transactions from the Provincial Accounting Office and analyze and reconcile the noted discrepancies amounting P46,491.04 and P898,574.91, respectively between the inter-agency accounts.
Transactions affecting reciprocal accounts were monitored. As a result, the account balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds (2-03-01-010) and Due to Other Funds (1-03-04-050) were reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Trace the variance of P154,350.00 between the Subsidiary Ledger and Cashbook balances and, if warranted, draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to effect the necessary adjustment to accounts; and
b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their respective books to ensure the correctness of the balance reported as Cash in Bank – LCCA, in compliance with Section 111 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
JEV No. 300-2022-12-0308 was drawn to effect the necessary adjustment to correct the discrepancy noted between the SL and the Cashbook balances. In support, a copy of Disbursement Voucher No. 300-2022-12-0314 dated December 12, 2022 pertaining to the payment of the Expanded Newborn Screening Cards amounting to P154,350.00 was furnished to the Audit Team.
Regular reconciliation is being conducted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to adjust and/or correct errors in the Cash in Bank – LCCA account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on matters relating to the accounting policy adopted for the treatment of borrowing costs pursuant to Paragraph 16 of IPSAS 5.
observations.managementAction: To comply with the audit recommendation, the Municipal Accountant requested the amended the submitted Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the 177 DVs and their supporting documents totaling P33,628,196.17 to avoid audit suspension of the same, which could mature into disallowance and henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of Section 7.2.1a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the timely submission of the DVs.
observations.managementAction: As compliance to the issued Notice of Suspension (NS) Nos. 23-001-100-(22), 23-002-200-(22) and 23-003-300-(22) dated July 11, 2023 of the Audit Team, the Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) submitted the 177 DVs with a total amount of P33,628,196.17 to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately present to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to support the recording of Accounts Payable; otherwise, the Municipal Accountant shall draw an adjusting JEV to revert to unappropriated surplus the undocumented Accounts Payable.
Likewise, the Municipality should ensure that all supporting documents are attached to prove that there are actual deliveries of goods or services were rendered.
Henceforth, ensure that all supporting documents are attached to respective claims.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant drew the following JEVs to revert the AP with lacking supporting documents to unappropriated surplus:
• 100-2023-05-0040/ May 26, 2023
• 100-2023-05-0041/ May 30, 2023
• 100-2023-05-0042/ May 30, 2023
Additionally, the AP amounting P2,494,255.25 were paid during CY 2023 and the requested supporting documents were attached therein.
Copies of the Delivery Receipts and other documents that supports actual delivery of the goods and services rendered were duly attached to the respective claims.

observations.recommendation: Prospectively, we recommended that the general revision shall be undertaken every three years as prescribed by Section 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The Management, through the Municipal Assessor, provided the Audit Team with a copy of the Proposed Schedule of Base Unit Market Values for residential, commercial and industrial lands duly received by the Provincial Assessor’s Office on February 24, 2020 for review and approval of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
The imposition of the general revision of real property assessment is on a provincial level, the Municipal Assessor can only continuously coordinate with its Provincial counterpart to fast track the revisions on the said assessment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality consider adopting the APS for processing and payment of wages of personnel under JO and COS to improve the efficiency of disbursement processing and to ensure that the risk associated with manual handling of cash such as loss thru misappropriation is mitigated.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2023, wages for 359 Job Order (JO) employees and 98 Contract of Service (COS) employees were processed through the APS. The Municipality implemented the APS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to ensure that the uninstalled steel posts are stored inside or under cover and in a clean and dry environment to avoid the risk of distortion/deterioration. We further recommended that the MDRRMO submit the detailed engineering and target timeline of the proposed installation of the 38 steel posts for the streetlights/camera in Calumpang-Nagcarlan Road. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Head of Procuring Entity, direct the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that end-users or implementing units sufficiently undertake detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs prior to bidding and award of contracts pursuant to Section 17.6 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
The steel posts were utilized during Calendar Year (CY) 2023 as part of the Municipality’s project – Installation of Streetlights. Detailed engineering investigations, surveys, and designs are being conducted by the Municipality prior to bidding and awarding contracts.
Henceforth, end-users will undertake detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs prior to bidding and award of contracts. The project installation has been complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete Listing of Road Network with description and cost segregation of road components for road projects and provide the same to the OIC – Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO);
observations.managementAction: Preparation of Road Network was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. OIC – Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) and Stock Cards (SCs), respectively;
observations.managementAction: The GSO Office has no stock. We immediately distributed the supplies immediately to the concerned offices and departments. SLCs and SCs were still not maintained by the Municipal Accountant and GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer, at the beginning of the year, to furnish the OIC – Municipal Accountant the updated duly certified list of Real Property Tax (RPT) Certified List of RPT Delinquency Treasury Office send a copy of List of RPT Delinquency and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the year. Thereafter, based on the submitted list, direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables and its corresponding deferred income account by drawing the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
observations.managementAction: At the beginning of the year, the Treasury Office did not furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of RPT Delinquency. The Municipality did not furnish the updated certified Statement of Delinquency submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office amounting to P14,995,220.67 and make the necessary adjustments in the RPT/SET Receivables accounts and its Deferred Income by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if warranted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: a. trace the material variance of P5,206,113.32 between the Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) and Cashbook balances and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment to accounts; and b. conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their respective books to ensure the correctness of the balance reported as Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current and Savings Accounts, in compliance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445; and c. provide a written justification on the noted repeated non-compliance with the previous years’ audit recommendation; otherwise, administrative sanctions shall be imposed pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: To conduct reconciliation of records and SL and Cashbook balances. Ensure that the balances are balanced and reconciled as to bank. Submitted justification.
observations.reason: Ensure that the balances (SL and Cashbook) are balanced and reconciled as to bank. Continuous monitoring by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Certified List of RPT Delinquency; and (b) based on the submitted list, direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables and its corresponding deferred income account by drawing the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
observations.managementAction: Ensure that the Certified List of RPT Delinquency of the Treasury Office is submitted to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year. The Municipal Treasurer to send a copy of Certified List of RPT Delinquency to the OIC – Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding deferred income account. The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the payments be made through checks in compliance with Section 93 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, so as to enable the government to withhold taxes therefrom, if necessary. It is likewise recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer and the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure strict compliance with the rules, regulations, policies and procedural guidelines provided in Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 on the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate GSO service provider that check be accepted as the mode of procurement. Coordinate the service provider that check be accepted as the mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officers to liquidate all cash advances at year-end and comply strictly with item 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002, taking in to consideration the requirement of Item 5.9 of the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances taking into consideration the requirement of Item 5.9 of the same Circular.
observations.reason: The Advances for Operating Expenses were liquidated on Dec.29, 2022-Feb.28, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to exert effort to collect the subject receivables amounting to P3,420,891.17. If these efforts prove futile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission to the COA of the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables together with the necessary documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials to institute measures, including the imposition of sanctions on negligent officials, to ensure that all receivables of the Municipal are collected and cash advances are liquidated on due dates.
observations.managementAction: Send 2nd Demand Letter to Accountable Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to conduct thorough review and analysis of subsidiary records for advances and other receivables. Consider that information gathered from such undertaking as basis for gathering certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions of the accounts, such as (i) death certificate issued by the PSA; (ii) proof of insolvency; (iii) proof of collection efforts, etc. Evaluate result of the process and determine if request for write-off from the Commission on Audit is necessary. Henceforth, it is recommended that Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of subsidiary records of receivables, and cash advances, so as to immediately address accounts due for settlement.
observations.managementAction: Send 2nd Demand Letter to Accountable Officers. Consider information gathered from such undertaking as basis for gathering certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the approved Variation, Change, Extra Work, Suspension, Resumption Orders and other relevant documents to justify delays or the extension of contract time, otherwise, instruct the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages upon the subject contractors for unjustified delays incurred. Moreover, observe timeliness in the submission of infrastructure contracts including other required documents, in compliance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-01.
observations.managementAction: Prepare the required documents and submit supporting documents. Please see attached supporting documents.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer submitted the required documents to the Audit Team. See attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and the Chairman, Bids and Awards to address the causes of non-submission of Approved Contract and Delivery Report to the Office of the Auditor within five working days after its perfection/issuance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001. Likewise, furnish the Auditor with a copy of delivery documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate the timely conduct of inspection of the deliveries. Henceforth, comply strictly COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: Prepare all PO’s and contract PO’s and Contract then submit to COA. Submitted all completed PO's and contract PO's and Contract then submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Due to the distance of the COA Office the completed PO's and contract PO's and Contract were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to remit barangay share from real property tax within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160; and to remit provincial shares from real property and special education taxes immediately, to enable the maximum and timely utilization of funds by the province and barangays.
observations.managementAction: Prepare the remittance of barangay share from RPT. Remit the Barangay Share of RPT.
observations.reason: Remit the RPT Share of Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Inventory Committee (IC) to complete the reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE account balance to identify the cause of the variance necessary for the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustment to accounts; and b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain updated PPELC and PC, respectively, necessary for reconciliation of records. It is likewise recommended that the IC utilize the result of reconciliation of reports and records for PPE in the conduct of one-time-cleansing. Henceforth, conduct a complete cycle of physical count of PPE items owned by the Municipal Government of Santa Maria in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 80-124 dated January 18, 1980 and Manual on Property Custodianship.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee already reconciled the variances between RPCPPE and PPE account balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify completed and fully paid projects costing P11,723,357.32 from CIP to the corresponding PPE accounts by debiting Infrastructure Assets and Building and Other Structures and crediting CIP - Infrastructure Assets, and Building and Other Structures.
observations.managementAction: Draw Journal Entry Voucher to effect the reclassifying of fully paid project from CIP to the corresponding PPE accounts. The Municipal Accountant furnished the JEV to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant furnished the JEV to Audit Team copy of JEV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic review of subsidiary records for loans payable accounts so that the adjustments made will be taken up in their respective books to ensure the correctness of the balance reported as Loans Payable –Domestic accounts.
observations.managementAction: Conduct periodic review of subsidiary records for loans payable accounts and effect adjustments. The Municipal Accountant furnished the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) dated January 31, 2024 recognizing corrected balance of Loans Payable – Domestic account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officers to liquidate all cash advances at year-end and comply strictly with item 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002, taking in to consideration the requirement of Item 5.9 of the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances and comply strictly with item 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 taking in to consideration the requirement of Item 5.9 of the same Circular. The Advances for Operating Expenses were liquidated and adjusted on January 31, 2024. Send 2nd Demand Letter.
observations.reason: Advances for Operating Expenses were liquidated and adjusted on January 31, 2024. 2nd Demand Letter Sent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to exert effort to collect the subject receivables totaling P3,232,257.60. If these efforts prove futile, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission to the COA of the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables together with the necessary documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Send 2nd Demand Letter to Accountable Officer. If efforts prove futile, facilitate submission to COA for write-off.
observations.reason: We sent Second Demand Letter to one of the Accountable Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) grant cash advance for salaries, wages, allowances, honoraria and other similar payments equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period; and (b) subject the prepared payroll to review prior to its approval to ascertain the correctness of the same.
observations.managementAction: Review the prepared payroll and other similar documents prior to its approval to ascertain the correctness. Prepared payrolls were reviewed.
observations.reason: Reviewed payroll and other similar documents. Payrolls and other similar documents were prepared to ascertain the correctness.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances within the allowable period prescribed by COA Circular 97-002, otherwise, impose penalty which may include withholding of salary.
observations.managementAction: Require the accountable officers to liquidate cash advances within the allowable period prescribed by COA Circular 97-002; otherwise, impose penalty (withholding of salary). Send 2nd Demand Letter.
observations.reason: 2nd Demand Letter Sent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official, officer or employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance. It is likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate responsible disbursing officers to limit the cash advance handled by each DO/AO.
observations.managementAction: Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official, officer or employee without liquidating prior cash advance. Designate additional disbursing officers have been designated to limit the cash advance handled by each DO/AO.
observations.reason: Additional disbursing officers have been designated to limit the cash advance handled by each DO/AO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Treasurer to increase the bonding requirements of the Municipal Treasurer, Special Disbursing Officer and two Other Accountable Officers, pursuant to Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 and Treasury Order No. 11-2021.
observations.managementAction: Increase the bond of Municipal Treasurer, Special Disbursing Officer and two Other Accountable Officers. Treasury Office increased the budget allocation for bonding requirements.
observations.reason: The Treasury Office increased the budget allocation for the bonding of officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: a. Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement within the period prescribed under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and item 3.2 COA Circular No. 96-011, necessary to substantiate the difference of P891,739.49 between book and cash book, and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment to accounts; and b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected will be taken up in their respective books to ensure the correctness of the balance reported as Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled both the CIB – LCCA and cashbook records of Accountant and Treasurer. Submitted bank reconciliation statements. Already adjusted and reconciled.
observations.reason: Already adjusted and reconciled both records as of January 31, 2024 recognizing corrected balance of Loans Payable-Domestic account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare documents necessary for disposal such as (a) properly filled-up IIRUP, indicating therein the required information, such as appraised value, mode of disposition, etc., (b) property ledger card duly certified by the Municipal Accountant and submit to the Audit Team for inspection and review all the appraised value. It is likewise recommended that the MGSO conduct a periodic review of PPE records so as to establish the necessity of disposal. Henceforth, the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties has to be conducted in strictly compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Review the inventory record and inspect all the unserviceable motor vehicle and other property. Conduct proper documentation for disposal.
observations.reason: On going inventory and inspect all the unserviceable motor vehicle and other property. Not specified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Draw Journal Entry Voucher to effect adjustments to accounts; b. Ensure that the transactions are accurately and completely recorded by conducting adequate review of reports from source documents to produce accurate records/reports; and c. Conduct periodic review and analysis of accounts, which may include reconciliation of subsidiary records with the general leger. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 109 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Draw Journal Entry Voucher to effect the adjustments to accounts; Ensure that the transactions are accurately and completely recorded by conducting adequate review of reports from source documents to produce accurate records/reports; Conduct periodic review and analysis of accounts, which may include reconciliation of subsidiary records with the general ledger. Municipal Accountant furnished copy of JEV dated January 31, 2024 recognizing adjustment of affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Local Finance Committee to provide sufficient appropriation in the succeeding annual budget to facilitate transfer of title in the name of the Municipality; and b. Designate responsible officials who will facilitate in titling the lots or at least register with the RD available acquisition documents such as DOAS and TD. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 47 (c) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of the RRSPM. It is further recommended that the Municipality maintain a permanent record of land acquired for future reference.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the five paid Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of transactions and to avoid audit suspension of transactions, which could mature into disallowance. Henceforth, require submission of accounts within the period prescribed under PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Prepare Accounting Control and Vouchers were already submitted. The five paid Disbursement Vouchers were already submitted to the Office of the Auditor on the following dates: March 27, 2023, Oct.13, 2023, Nov.16, 2023, Jan.22, 2024 and Feb.20, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of all insurable property so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the General Service Insurance Fund. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, include in the annual budget sufficient appropriation for insurance expenses to ensure that all insurable property are covered by property insurance with the General Service Insurance Fund of the GSIS. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the proper implementation of programmed activities in accordance with the plan and budget.
observations.managementAction: Prepare and identify all the insurable properties of the LGU and renew every year. Identify all the properties of the Mayor. The Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of all insurable property so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the General Service Insurance Fund. Included in the CY 2024 budget the appropriation for property insurance.
observations.reason: No existing report. Failure to conduct BID Call for Rescue Vehicle. Some properties included in CY 2025 appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to remit barangay share from real property tax within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160; and to remit provincial shares from real property and special education taxes immediately, to enable the maximum and timely utilization of funds by the province and barangays.
observations.managementAction: Prepare the remittance of barangay share from RPT. Remittance was done. The Municipal Accountant furnished the Office of the Auditor a copy of JEV dated January 31, 2024 recognizing the corrected balance of Loans Payable-Domestic account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the accountable officer to deposit all collections promptly and intact with the Authorized Government Depository Bank to avoid the risk of loss or misuse of Municipality’s funds. Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of Section 69 of PD No. 1445, and Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: Draw Journal Entry Voucher to record deposits and ensure collections are deposited promptly and intact with the Authorized Government Depository Bank. The Municipal Accountant furnished the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) dated January 31, 2024 recognizing RPT shares.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. draw JEV to effect necessary adjustments to the accounts; and b. observe caution in recording transactions by conducting adequate review of reports from source documents to produce accurate records/reports. Henceforth, record all transactions based on valid documents compliance with Section 112 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Draw Journal Entry Voucher to effect the necessary adjustments to the accounts. Municipal Accountant furnished the Office of the Auditor a copy of the JEV dated January 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor the MDRRMO: a. Adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP for effective utilization of LDRRMF; and b. Facilitate the early preparation/formulation of LDRRMFIP and other related reports in order that the planned PPAs are immediately implemented and assure the attainment of the Municipal’s objective of mitigating the impacts of disaster/calamities towards building a disaster-resilient community. Likewise, execute/undertake proper planning to ensure that all PPAs as provided in the LDRRMFIP are fully executed.
observations.managementAction: Adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP for effective utilization of the fund. Facilitate the early preparation/formulation of LDRRMFIP and related reports. High river water level affected River Dredging. Acquisition of Rescue Vehicle: call for bid failed to be conducted due to failure to bid. Implemented some activities.
observations.reason: Failure to conduct BID Call for Rescue Vehicle. High river water level where the project will be conducted. Some PPAs conducted, others on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the MDRRMO to stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 described above; and b. Refund from the General Fund to the Trust Fund – Trust Liability- DRRM the amount of P523,874.81 representing the inappropriate utilization of the LDRRMF to programs/activities that do not fall under the specifications provided under the law and statutes and submit to this Office the journal entry voucher and the disbursement voucher of the said refund, otherwise, such will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Stop the utilization of LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities that are not related to the DRRM activities as provided under the JMCs. DRRMO PPA’s were linked to Completed operations and information dissemination, mobilization of ERT; monitoring, warning and early warning system. Request for refund to be evaluated. Some PPAs were related to disaster operations.
observations.reason: PPA’s were linked to the Completed operations and information dissemination. Justification submitted for evaluation by Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to: a. Appropriate and utilize the 20 percent DF for PPAs that contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and b. Stop the practice of charging regular expenditures against the 20 percent Development Fund, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Appropriate and utilize the 20 percent DF for PPAs that contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development outcomes and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures. Adjustments to be considered. Some PPAs were justified as used for Farm to Market Road rehabilitation; labor component included in program of works.
observations.reason: Some justifications provided; items to be validated. Ongoing validation. Must comply with JMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the COA Team of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions. observations.managementAction: Prepare the required documents and submit to the COA Team. Original documents already submitted to the COA Team.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that Management refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursements through reimbursement procedure. All payments shall be made through checks, in compliance with Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002, so as to enable the government to withhold taxes therefrom. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the COA Team of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions. Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 on the processing, approval and payments of claims.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up the responsible officials regarding the Mayor’s Business Permit and Certification of Registration of Philgeps Registration. Submitted supporting documents on Dec.18, 2023. One DV with check no.722711 was already submitted with complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal General Services Officer to: a. Cause the updating of PAR of PPE issued to Municipality’s Officers and Employees; and b. Issue renewal of issued PARs every three years or during transfer of accountability. Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirement of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.reason: Update PAR of PPE to be issued to accountable employee. PARs not yet updated; subject for continuous monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare RCI summarizing therein all issued and cancelled checks. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions stated to Sections 55 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) summarizing all issued and cancelled checks. In March 2024, the prescribed format of RCI was prepared and used.
observations.reason: In March 2024, the prescribed format of RCI was used in the preparation of reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor all monthly and quarterly financial reports; and (b) submit year-end financial statements and schedules to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1, Section 41.2 of PD No. 1445 and item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.
observations.managementAction: Prepare and submit monthly and quarterly financial reports. The Municipal Accountant submitted the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules to the Audit Team in February and March 2024. Submission of various reports is completed. Some monthly and quarterly reports were submitted late; year-end statements were submitted within February - March 2024.
observations.reason: Submission of year-end statements and schedules was done after February 14; validation will be conducted at the end of CY 2024. Continuous monitoring as recommended.

observations.recommendation: Management issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance after the defects liability period, which is one year from the date of project completion pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to protect the Municipality from incurring unnecessary additional cost for any damage to the infrastructure project on account of the use of materials of inferior quality or structural defects.

BAC ensure that the detailed engineering is carefully and sufficiently prepared before undertaking any procurement activity by instituting necessary remedial measures in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR.

Follow the Cebu SP Ordinance No. 2008-10 on the proper application of tax discounts for advance/prompt payment.

Management prioritize the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are identified in the SWM plan and provide adequate funding to effectively implement relevant PPAs relative to solid waste management.

Management, through the Municipality’s LDRRM Office, provide written explanation on charging the subject expenditures to LDRRM Fund instead of General Fund.

Direct the MHRMO to actively publish the vacancy of the newly-created position.

The Municipal Budget Officer to review the budget and plantilla positions of the Municipality and coordinate with Sangguniang Bayan to create the remaining one mandated position of the LDRRMO pursuant to Section 5 of JMC No. 2014-1 so that efficient and effective discharge of the functions of LDRRM office can be attained.

Direct the concerned personnel to refund the excess per diem claimed for the activity. Subsequently, refrain from making payments on expenses without proper supporting documents and observe the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in claiming of travel allowance.

Expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the Municipality’s projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

Municipal Accountant to account for the over remittance, and take necessary actions to recover the funds such as filing of tax credits or refund, if necessary.

Limit the disbursements within the amount appropriated to avoid incurrence of overdraft.

Establish clear cut guidelines on the selection and grant of AIFCS which may be aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.  

Explanation on why travel expenses for Job Order workers were charged to the appropriation for AIFCS.

To use a portion of the GAD budget for payment of professional fees, honoraria and other services for gender experts or gender specialists engaged to strengthen GFPS capability by updating and enhancing their skills through continuing gender education customized according to their functions to fully equip the GFPS in the formulation of their annual GPBs within the context of their mandates and other GAD processes to ensure gender mainstreaming in their policies, programs and projects.

Secure updated monitoring report from the Dept. of Agriculture on the projects so that the same can be validated.
